14	"""Constrained decoding with outlines backend."""
15	
16	import json
17	import logging
18	from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Tuple, Union
19	
20	import interegular
21	import torch
22	from outlines.fsm.guide import RegexGuide
23	from outlines.models.transformers import TransformerTokenizer
24	from pydantic import BaseModel
25	
26	from sglang.srt.constrained.base_grammar_backend import (
27	    INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ,
28	    BaseGrammarBackend,
29	    BaseGrammarObject,
30	)
31	from sglang.srt.constrained.outlines_jump_forward import OutlinesJumpForwardMap
32	
33	try:
34	    from outlines.fsm.json_schema import build_regex_from_schema
35	except ImportError:
36	    from outlines_core.fsm.json_schema import build_regex_from_schema
37	
38	
39	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
40	
41	
42	class OutlinesGrammar(BaseGrammarObject):
43	    def __init__(
44	        self,
45	        guide: RegexGuide,
46	        jump_forward_map: Union[OutlinesJumpForwardMap, None],
47	    ) -> None:
48	        super().__init__()
49	        self.guide = guide
50	        self.jump_forward_map = jump_forward_map
51	        self.state = 0
52	
53	    def accept_token(self, token: int):
54	        self.state = self.guide.get_next_state(self.state, token)
55	
56	    def allocate_vocab_mask(
57	        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
58	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
59	        return torch.zeros(batch_size, vocab_size, dtype=torch.bool, device=device)
60	
61	    @staticmethod
62	    def move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor:
63	        return vocab_mask
64	
65	    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
66	        tokens = torch.tensor(
67	            self.guide.get_next_instruction(self.state).tokens, dtype=torch.int64
68	        ).to(vocab_mask.device, non_blocking=True)
69	        vocab_mask = vocab_mask[idx]
70	        vocab_mask.fill_(1)
71	        vocab_mask.scatter_(0, tokens, torch.zeros_like(tokens, dtype=torch.bool))
72	
73	    @staticmethod
74	    def apply_vocab_mask(logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor):
75	        logits.masked_fill_(vocab_mask, float("-inf"))
76	
77	    def copy(self):
78	        return OutlinesGrammar(self.guide, self.jump_forward_map)
79	
80	    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer) -> Optional[Tuple]:
81	        if not self.jump_forward_map:
82	            return None
83	
84	        jump_forward_bytes = self.jump_forward_map.jump_forward_byte(self.state)
85	        if jump_forward_bytes is None or len(jump_forward_bytes) <= 1:
86	            return None
87	
88	        # preprocess the jump forward string
89	        suffix_bytes = []
90	        continuation_range = range(0x80, 0xC0)
91	        cur_state = self.state
92	        while (
93	            len(jump_forward_bytes) and jump_forward_bytes[0][0] in continuation_range
94	        ):
95	            # continuation bytes
96	            byte_edge = jump_forward_bytes.pop(0)
97	            suffix_bytes.append(byte_edge[0])
98	            cur_state = byte_edge[1]
99	
100	        suffix_tokens = [f"<0x{hex(b)[2:].upper()}>" for b in suffix_bytes]
101	        suffix_ids = tokenizer.convert_tokens_to_ids(suffix_tokens)
102	        return suffix_ids, cur_state
103	
104	    def jump_forward_str_state(self, helper: Tuple[List[int], str]) -> Tuple[str, int]:
105	        _, cur_state = helper
106	        return self.jump_forward_map.jump_forward_symbol(cur_state)
107	
108	    def jump_and_retokenize(
109	        self, old_output_ids: List[int], new_output_ids: List[int], next_state: int
110	    ):
111	        self.state = next_state
112	
113	
114	class OutlinesGrammarBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
115	    def __init__(
116	        self,
117	        tokenizer,
118	        whitespace_pattern: str | None,
119	    ):
120	        super().__init__()
121	
122	        try:
123	            self.outlines_tokenizer = TransformerTokenizer(tokenizer)
124	        except AttributeError:
125	            # FIXME: tmp fix for chatglm2 & chatglm3 (pad_token_id=0)
126	            origin_pad_token_id = tokenizer.pad_token_id
127	
128	            def fset(self, value):
129	                self._value = value
130	
131	            type(tokenizer).pad_token_id = property(
132	                fget=type(tokenizer).pad_token_id.fget, fset=fset
133	            )
134	            self.outlines_tokenizer = TransformerTokenizer(tokenizer)
135	            self.outlines_tokenizer.tokenizer.pad_token_id = origin_pad_token_id
136	            self.outlines_tokenizer.pad_token_id = origin_pad_token_id
137	            self.outlines_tokenizer.pad_token = (
138	                self.outlines_tokenizer.tokenizer.pad_token
139	            )
140	            self.outlines_tokenizer.vocabulary = (
141	                self.outlines_tokenizer.tokenizer.get_vocab()
142	            )
143	        self.whitespace_pattern = whitespace_pattern
144	
145	    def _compile_regex(self, regex: str) -> Optional[OutlinesGrammar]:
146	        try:
147	            if hasattr(RegexGuide, "from_regex"):
148	                # outlines >= 0.1.1
149	                guide = RegexGuide.from_regex(regex, self.outlines_tokenizer)
150	            else:
151	                # outlines <= 0.0.46
152	                guide = RegexGuide(regex, self.outlines_tokenizer)
153	        except interegular.patterns.InvalidSyntax as e:
154	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid regex schema: {regex=}, {e=}")
155	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
156	
157	        jump_forward_map = None
158	        return OutlinesGrammar(guide, jump_forward_map)
159	
160	    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str):
161	        return super().dispatch_ebnf(key_string)
162	
163	    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str):
164	        return super().dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)
165	
166	    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str):
167	        try:
168	            regex = build_regex_from_object(
169	                key_string,
170	                whitespace_pattern=self.whitespace_pattern,
171	            )
172	        except (NotImplementedError, json.decoder.JSONDecodeError, ValueError) as e:
173	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid json_schema: {key_string=}, {e=}")
174	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
175	        return self._compile_regex(regex)
176	
177	    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str):
178	        return self._compile_regex(key_string)
179	
180	
181	def build_regex_from_object(
182	    object: Union[str, BaseModel, Dict], whitespace_pattern: Optional[str] = None
183	):
184	    if isinstance(object, type(BaseModel)):
185	        schema = json.dumps(object.model_json_schema())
186	    elif isinstance(object, Dict):
187	        schema = json.dumps(object)
188	    else:
189	        schema = object
190	    return build_regex_from_schema(schema, whitespace_pattern)
191

14	"""Constrained decoding with outlines backend."""
15	
16	import json
17	import logging
18	from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Tuple, Union
19	
20	import interegular
21	import torch
22	from outlines.fsm.guide import RegexGuide
23	from outlines.models.transformers import TransformerTokenizer
24	from pydantic import BaseModel
25	
26	from sglang.srt.constrained.base_grammar_backend import (
27	    INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ,
28	    BaseGrammarBackend,
29	    BaseGrammarObject,
30	)
31	from sglang.srt.constrained.outlines_jump_forward import OutlinesJumpForwardMap
32	
33	try:
34	    from outlines.fsm.json_schema import build_regex_from_schema
35	except ImportError:
36	    from outlines_core.fsm.json_schema import build_regex_from_schema
37	
38	
39	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
40	
41	
42	class OutlinesGrammar(BaseGrammarObject):
43	    def __init__(
44	        self,
45	        guide: RegexGuide,
46	        jump_forward_map: Union[OutlinesJumpForwardMap, None],
47	    ) -> None:
48	        super().__init__()
49	        self.guide = guide
50	        self.jump_forward_map = jump_forward_map
51	        self.state = 0
52	
53	    def accept_token(self, token: int):
54	        self.state = self.guide.get_next_state(self.state, token)
55	
56	    def allocate_vocab_mask(
57	        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
58	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
59	        return torch.zeros(batch_size, vocab_size, dtype=torch.bool, device=device)
60	
61	    @staticmethod
62	    def move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor:
63	        return vocab_mask
64	
65	    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
66	        tokens = torch.tensor(
67	            self.guide.get_next_instruction(self.state).tokens, dtype=torch.int64
68	        ).to(vocab_mask.device, non_blocking=True)
69	        vocab_mask = vocab_mask[idx]
70	        vocab_mask.fill_(1)
71	        vocab_mask.scatter_(0, tokens, torch.zeros_like(tokens, dtype=torch.bool))
72	
73	    @staticmethod
74	    def apply_vocab_mask(logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor):
75	        logits.masked_fill_(vocab_mask, float("-inf"))
76	
77	    def copy(self):
78	        return OutlinesGrammar(self.guide, self.jump_forward_map)
79	
80	    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer) -> Optional[Tuple]:
81	        if not self.jump_forward_map:
82	            return None
83	
84	        jump_forward_bytes = self.jump_forward_map.jump_forward_byte(self.state)
85	        if jump_forward_bytes is None or len(jump_forward_bytes) <= 1:
86	            return None
87	
88	        # preprocess the jump forward string
89	        suffix_bytes = []
90	        continuation_range = range(0x80, 0xC0)
91	        cur_state = self.state
92	        while (
93	            len(jump_forward_bytes) and jump_forward_bytes[0][0] in continuation_range
94	        ):
95	            # continuation bytes
96	            byte_edge = jump_forward_bytes.pop(0)
97	            suffix_bytes.append(byte_edge[0])
98	            cur_state = byte_edge[1]
99	
100	        suffix_tokens = [f"<0x{hex(b)[2:].upper()}>" for b in suffix_bytes]
101	        suffix_ids = tokenizer.convert_tokens_to_ids(suffix_tokens)
102	        return suffix_ids, cur_state
103	
104	    def jump_forward_str_state(self, helper: Tuple[List[int], str]) -> Tuple[str, int]:
105	        _, cur_state = helper
106	        return self.jump_forward_map.jump_forward_symbol(cur_state)
107	
108	    def jump_and_retokenize(
109	        self, old_output_ids: List[int], new_output_ids: List[int], next_state: int
110	    ):
111	        self.state = next_state
112	
113

42	def run_internal(state, program, func_args, func_kwargs, sync):
43	    try:
44	        state.ret_value = program.func(state, *func_args, **func_kwargs)
45	    except Exception as e:
46	        raise e
47	    finally:
48	        state.stream_executor.end()
49	
50	    if sync:
51	        state.stream_executor.sync()
52	
53	    if global_config.verbosity >= 2:
54	        print(state.text())
55	
56	
57	def run_program(
58	    program,
59	    backend,
60	    func_args,
61	    func_kwargs,
62	    default_sampling_para,
63	    stream,
64	    sync=False,
65	    use_thread=True,
66	):
67	    if hasattr(backend, "endpoint"):
68	        backend = backend.endpoint
69	    assert backend is not None, "Please specify a backend"
70	    func_kwargs.update(program.bind_arguments)
71	    stream_executor = StreamExecutor(
72	        backend,
73	        func_kwargs,
74	        default_sampling_para,
75	        chat_template=None,
76	        stream=stream,
77	        num_api_spec_tokens=program.num_api_spec_tokens,
78	        use_thread=use_thread,
79	    )
80	    state = ProgramState(stream_executor)
81	
82	    if stream:
83	        t = threading.Thread(
84	            target=run_internal, args=(state, program, func_args, func_kwargs, sync)
85	        )
86	        t.start()
87	        return state
88	    else:
89	        run_internal(state, program, func_args, func_kwargs, sync)
90	        return state

14	"""Constrained decoding with xgrammar backend."""
15	
16	import dataclasses
17	import json
18	import logging
19	from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Tuple, Union
20	
21	import torch
22	from xgrammar import (
23	    CompiledGrammar,
24	    GrammarCompiler,
25	    GrammarMatcher,
26	    StructuralTagItem,
27	    TokenizerInfo,
28	    allocate_token_bitmask,
29	)
30	
31	from sglang.srt.constrained.base_grammar_backend import (
32	    INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ,
33	    BaseGrammarBackend,
34	    BaseGrammarObject,
35	    GrammarStats,
36	)
37	from sglang.srt.constrained.utils import is_legacy_structural_tag
38	from sglang.srt.utils import is_hip
39	
40	_is_hip = is_hip()
41	if _is_hip:
42	    from sgl_kernel import apply_token_bitmask_inplace_cuda
43	else:
44	    from sglang.srt.constrained.triton_ops.bitmask_ops import (
45	        apply_token_bitmask_inplace_triton,
46	    )
47	
48	
49	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
50	MAX_ROLLBACK_TOKENS = 200
51	
52	
53	class XGrammarGrammar(BaseGrammarObject):
54	
55	    def __init__(
56	        self,
57	        matcher: GrammarMatcher,
58	        vocab_size: int,
59	        ctx: CompiledGrammar,
60	        override_stop_tokens: Optional[Union[List[int], int]],
61	        key_string: Optional[str] = None,  # TODO (sk): for debugging, remove later
62	        grammar_stats: Optional[GrammarStats] = GrammarStats(),
63	    ) -> None:
64	        super().__init__()
65	        self.matcher = matcher
66	        self.vocab_size = vocab_size
67	        self.ctx = ctx
68	        self.override_stop_tokens = override_stop_tokens
69	        self.accepted_tokens = []
70	        self.key_string = key_string
71	        self.grammar_stats = grammar_stats
72	
73	    def accept_token(self, token: int):
74	        if not self.is_terminated():
75	            self.current_token = token
76	            accepted = self.matcher.accept_token(token)
77	            if not accepted:
78	                # log for debugging
79	                raise ValueError(
80	                    f"Tokens not accepted: {token}\n"
81	                    f"Accepted tokens: {self.accepted_tokens}\n"
82	                    f"Key string: {self.key_string}"
83	                )
84	            else:
85	                self.accepted_tokens.append(token)
86	
87	    def rollback(self, k: int):
88	        self.matcher.rollback(k)
89	        self.accepted_tokens = self.accepted_tokens[:-k]
90	
91	    def is_terminated(self):
92	        return self.matcher.is_terminated()
93	
94	    def allocate_vocab_mask(
95	        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
96	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
97	        return allocate_token_bitmask(batch_size, vocab_size)
98	
99	    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
100	        self.matcher.fill_next_token_bitmask(vocab_mask, idx)
101	
102	    @staticmethod
103	    def move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor:
104	        return vocab_mask.to(device, non_blocking=True)
105	
106	    def apply_vocab_mask(self, logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor) -> None:
107	        if (
108	            logits.device.type == "cuda"
109	            or logits.device.type == "npu"
110	            or logits.device.type == "xpu"
111	        ):
112	            if _is_hip:
113	                apply_token_bitmask_inplace_cuda(logits, vocab_mask)
114	            else:
115	                apply_token_bitmask_inplace_triton(logits, vocab_mask)
116	        elif logits.device.type == "cpu" and self.apply_vocab_mask_cpu:
117	            self.apply_vocab_mask_cpu(logits, vocab_mask)
118	        else:
119	            raise RuntimeError(f"Unsupported device: {logits.device.type}")
120	
121	    def copy(self):
122	        matcher = GrammarMatcher(
123	            self.ctx,
124	            max_rollback_tokens=MAX_ROLLBACK_TOKENS,
125	            override_stop_tokens=self.override_stop_tokens,
126	        )
127	        return XGrammarGrammar(
128	            matcher,
129	            self.vocab_size,
130	            self.ctx,
131	            self.override_stop_tokens,
132	            self.key_string,
133	            dataclasses.replace(
134	                self.grammar_stats, is_cache_hit=True, tree_traversal_time=[]
135	            ),
136	        )
137	
138	    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer) -> Optional[Tuple[List[int], str]]:
139	        s = self.matcher.find_jump_forward_string()
140	        if s:
141	            return [], s
142	        return None
143	
144	    def jump_forward_str_state(self, helper: Tuple[List[int], str]) -> Tuple[str, int]:
145	        _, data = helper
146	        return data, -1
147	
148	    def jump_and_retokenize(
149	        self, old_output_ids: List[int], new_output_ids: List[int], next_state: int
150	    ):
151	        k = 0
152	        for i, old_id in enumerate(old_output_ids):
153	            if old_id == new_output_ids[i]:
154	                k = i + 1
155	            else:
156	                break
157	
158	        # rollback to the last token that is the same
159	        if k < len(old_output_ids):
160	            self.matcher.rollback(len(old_output_ids) - k)
161	
162	        for i in range(k, len(new_output_ids)):
163	            assert self.matcher.accept_token(new_output_ids[i])
164	
165	    def __repr__(self):
166	        return f"XGrammarGrammar({self.key_string=}, {self.accepted_tokens=}, {self.current_token=})"
167	
168	
169	class TokenizerNotSupportedError(Exception):
170	    """Raised when tokenizer is not supported by XGrammar backend."""
171	
172	    pass
173	
174	
175	class XGrammarGrammarBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
176	    def __init__(
177	        self,
178	        tokenizer,
179	        vocab_size: int,
180	        model_eos_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
181	        any_whitespace: bool = True,
182	    ):
183	        super().__init__()
184	
185	        if hasattr(tokenizer, "init_xgrammar"):
186	            # For special tokenizer
187	            tokenizer_info, override_stop_tokens = tokenizer.init_xgrammar()
188	
189	            if tokenizer_info is None:
190	                # Not supported tokenizer
191	                raise TokenizerNotSupportedError(
192	                    f"Tokenizer type {type(tokenizer).__name__} is not supported by XGrammar"
193	                )
194	        else:
195	            # Create TokenizerInfo with model's EOS tokens as the authoritative stop tokens
196	            # This ensures consistency between what the model considers EOS and what XGrammar uses
197	            try:
198	                tokenizer_info = TokenizerInfo.from_huggingface(
199	                    tokenizer, vocab_size=vocab_size, stop_token_ids=model_eos_token_ids
200	                )
201	                override_stop_tokens = None
202	            except Exception as e:
203	                raise TokenizerNotSupportedError(
204	                    f"Failed to create XGrammar TokenizerInfo from tokenizer: {e}"
205	                )
206	
207	        self.grammar_compiler = GrammarCompiler(tokenizer_info=tokenizer_info)
208	        self.vocab_size = vocab_size
209	        self.override_stop_tokens = override_stop_tokens
210	        self.any_whitespace = any_whitespace
211	
212	    @staticmethod
213	    def _sanitize_structural_format(structural_format):
214	        """Recursively replace missing json_schema fields with an empty schema."""
215	        if not isinstance(structural_format, dict):
216	            return
217	
218	        fmt_type = structural_format.get("type")
219	        if fmt_type in {"json_schema", "qwen_xml_parameter"}:
220	            if structural_format.get("json_schema") is None:
221	                structural_format["json_schema"] = {}
222	
223	        if fmt_type == "tag":
224	            XGrammarGrammarBackend._sanitize_structural_format(
225	                structural_format.get("content")
226	            )
227	        elif fmt_type in {"sequence", "or"}:
228	            for element in structural_format.get("elements", []):
229	                XGrammarGrammarBackend._sanitize_structural_format(element)
230	        elif fmt_type in {"triggered_tags", "tags_with_separator"}:
231	            for tag in structural_format.get("tags", []):
232	                XGrammarGrammarBackend._sanitize_structural_format(tag)
233	
234	    @staticmethod
235	    def _sanitize_structural_tag_structures(structural_tag: Dict) -> None:
236	        for structure in structural_tag.get("structures", []):
237	            if structure.get("schema") is None:
238	                structure["schema"] = {}

240	    def _from_context(
241	        self, ctx: CompiledGrammar, key_string: str, grammar_stats: GrammarStats
242	    ) -> XGrammarGrammar:
243	        matcher = GrammarMatcher(
244	            ctx,
245	            max_rollback_tokens=MAX_ROLLBACK_TOKENS,
246	            override_stop_tokens=self.override_stop_tokens,
247	        )
248	        return XGrammarGrammar(
249	            matcher,
250	            self.vocab_size,
251	            ctx,
252	            self.override_stop_tokens,
253	            key_string,
254	            grammar_stats,
255	        )
256	
257	    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
258	        try:
259	            if key_string == "$$ANY$$":
260	                # Note: This builtin JSON grammar includes *all* valid JSON (including, for example, arrays at the root)
261	                ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_builtin_json_grammar()
262	            else:
263	                ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_json_schema(
264	                    schema=key_string, any_whitespace=self.any_whitespace
265	                )
266	
267	        except (RuntimeError, json.decoder.JSONDecodeError, UnicodeDecodeError) as e:
268	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid json_schema: {key_string=}, {e=}")
269	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
270	        return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="json"))
271	
272	    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
273	        try:
274	            ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_grammar(key_string)
275	        except RuntimeError as e:
276	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid ebnf: {key_string=}, {e=}")
277	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
278	        return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="ebnf"))
279	
280	    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
281	        try:
282	            ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_regex(key_string)
283	        except RuntimeError as e:
284	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid regex: {key_string=}, {e=}")
285	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
286	        return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="regex"))
287	
288	    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
289	        try:

291	            structural_tag = json.loads(key_string)
292	            if is_legacy_structural_tag(structural_tag):
293	                self._sanitize_structural_tag_structures(structural_tag)
294	                tags = [

296	                        begin=structure["begin"],

298	                        end=structure["end"],
299	                    )
300	                    for structure in structural_tag["structures"]

828	class ProgramState:
829	    """The state of an SGL program."""
830	
831	    def __init__(self, stream_executor: StreamExecutor):
832	        self.stream_executor = stream_executor
833	
834	    def _role_common(self, name: str, expr: Optional[SglExpr] = None):
835	        if expr is not None:
836	            role_expr = SglExprList([SglRoleBegin(name), expr, SglRoleEnd(name)])
837	            self.stream_executor.submit(role_expr)
838	            return role_expr
839	        else:
840	
841	            @contextmanager
842	            def role_scope():
843	                self.stream_executor.submit(SglRoleBegin(name))
844	                yield
845	                self.stream_executor.submit(SglRoleEnd(name))
846	
847	            return role_scope()
848	
849	    def system(self, expr: Optional[SglExpr] = None):
850	        return self._role_common("system", expr)
851	
852	    def user(self, expr: Optional[SglExpr] = None):
853	        return self._role_common("user", expr)
854	
855	    def assistant(self, expr: Optional[SglExpr] = None):
856	        return self._role_common("assistant", expr)
857	
858	    @contextmanager
859	    def var_scope(self, name: str):
860	        self.stream_executor.submit(SglVarScopeBegin(name))
861	        yield
862	        self.stream_executor.submit(SglVarScopeEnd(name))
863	
864	    def fork(
865	        self,
866	        size: int = 1,
867	        position_ids_offset: Optional[List[int]] = None,
868	    ):
869	        stream_executors = self.stream_executor.fork(size, position_ids_offset)
870	        states = [ProgramState(x) for x in stream_executors]
871	        state_group = ProgramStateGroup(states, self)
872	        return state_group
873	
874	    @contextmanager
875	    def copy(self, position_ids_offset: Optional[List[int]] = None):
876	        state_group = self.fork(1, position_ids_offset)
877	        try:
878	            yield state_group[0]
879	        finally:
880	            state_group.join()
881	
882	    def text(self):
883	        return self.stream_executor.text()
884	
885	    def messages(self):
886	        return self.stream_executor.messages()
887	
888	    def sync(self):
889	        return self.stream_executor.sync()

53	def convert_json_schema_to_str(json_schema: Union[dict, str, Type[BaseModel]]) -> str:
54	    """Convert a JSON schema to a string.
55	    Parameters
56	    ----------
57	    json_schema
58	        The JSON schema.
59	    Returns
60	    -------
61	    str
62	        The JSON schema converted to a string.
63	    Raises
64	    ------
65	    ValueError
66	        If the schema is not a dictionary, a string or a Pydantic class.
67	    """
68	    if isinstance(json_schema, dict):
69	        schema_str = json.dumps(json_schema)
70	    elif isinstance(json_schema, str):
71	        schema_str = json_schema
72	    elif issubclass(json_schema, BaseModel):
73	        schema_str = json.dumps(json_schema.model_json_schema())
74	    else:
75	        raise ValueError(
76	            f"Cannot parse schema {json_schema}. The schema must be either "
77	            + "a Pydantic class, a dictionary or a string that contains the JSON "
78	            + "schema specification"
79	        )
80	    return schema_str

89	def is_same_type(values: list):
90	    """Return whether the elements in values are of the same type."""
91	    if len(values) <= 1:
92	        return True
93	    else:
94	        t = type(values[0])
95	        return all(isinstance(v, t) for v in values[1:])

53	class XGrammarGrammar(BaseGrammarObject):
54	
55	    def __init__(
56	        self,
57	        matcher: GrammarMatcher,
58	        vocab_size: int,
59	        ctx: CompiledGrammar,
60	        override_stop_tokens: Optional[Union[List[int], int]],
61	        key_string: Optional[str] = None,  # TODO (sk): for debugging, remove later
62	        grammar_stats: Optional[GrammarStats] = GrammarStats(),
63	    ) -> None:
64	        super().__init__()
65	        self.matcher = matcher
66	        self.vocab_size = vocab_size
67	        self.ctx = ctx
68	        self.override_stop_tokens = override_stop_tokens
69	        self.accepted_tokens = []
70	        self.key_string = key_string
71	        self.grammar_stats = grammar_stats
72	
73	    def accept_token(self, token: int):
74	        if not self.is_terminated():
75	            self.current_token = token
76	            accepted = self.matcher.accept_token(token)
77	            if not accepted:
78	                # log for debugging
79	                raise ValueError(
80	                    f"Tokens not accepted: {token}\n"
81	                    f"Accepted tokens: {self.accepted_tokens}\n"
82	                    f"Key string: {self.key_string}"
83	                )
84	            else:
85	                self.accepted_tokens.append(token)
86	
87	    def rollback(self, k: int):
88	        self.matcher.rollback(k)
89	        self.accepted_tokens = self.accepted_tokens[:-k]
90	
91	    def is_terminated(self):
92	        return self.matcher.is_terminated()

148	    def jump_and_retokenize(
149	        self, old_output_ids: List[int], new_output_ids: List[int], next_state: int
150	    ):
151	        k = 0
152	        for i, old_id in enumerate(old_output_ids):
153	            if old_id == new_output_ids[i]:
154	                k = i + 1
155	            else:
156	                break
157	
158	        # rollback to the last token that is the same
159	        if k < len(old_output_ids):
160	            self.matcher.rollback(len(old_output_ids) - k)
161	
162	        for i in range(k, len(new_output_ids)):
163	            assert self.matcher.accept_token(new_output_ids[i])

832	        self.stream_executor = stream_executor
833	
834	    def _role_common(self, name: str, expr: Optional[SglExpr] = None):
835	        if expr is not None:
836	            role_expr = SglExprList([SglRoleBegin(name), expr, SglRoleEnd(name)])
837	            self.stream_executor.submit(role_expr)
838	            return role_expr
839	        else:
840	
841	            @contextmanager
842	            def role_scope():
843	                self.stream_executor.submit(SglRoleBegin(name))
844	                yield
845	                self.stream_executor.submit(SglRoleEnd(name))
846	
847	            return role_scope()
848	
849	    def system(self, expr: Optional[SglExpr] = None):
850	        return self._role_common("system", expr)
851	
852	    def user(self, expr: Optional[SglExpr] = None):
853	        return self._role_common("user", expr)
854	
855	    def assistant(self, expr: Optional[SglExpr] = None):
856	        return self._role_common("assistant", expr)
857	
858	    @contextmanager
859	    def var_scope(self, name: str):
860	        self.stream_executor.submit(SglVarScopeBegin(name))
861	        yield
862	        self.stream_executor.submit(SglVarScopeEnd(name))
863	
864	    def fork(
865	        self,
866	        size: int = 1,
867	        position_ids_offset: Optional[List[int]] = None,
868	    ):
869	        stream_executors = self.stream_executor.fork(size, position_ids_offset)
870	        states = [ProgramState(x) for x in stream_executors]
871	        state_group = ProgramStateGroup(states, self)
872	        return state_group
873	
874	    @contextmanager
875	    def copy(self, position_ids_offset: Optional[List[int]] = None):
876	        state_group = self.fork(1, position_ids_offset)
877	        try:
878	            yield state_group[0]
879	        finally:
880	            state_group.join()
881	
882	    def text(self):
883	        return self.stream_executor.text()
884

105	    def verify(self, vocab_size):
106	        if self.temperature < 0.0:
107	            raise ValueError(
108	                f"temperature must be non-negative, got {self.temperature}."
109	            )
110	        if not 0.0 < self.top_p <= 1.0:
111	            raise ValueError(f"top_p must be in (0, 1], got {self.top_p}.")
112	        if not 0.0 <= self.min_p <= 1.0:
113	            raise ValueError(f"min_p must be in [0, 1], got {self.min_p}.")
114	        if self.top_k < 1 or self.top_k == -1:
115	            raise ValueError(
116	                f"top_k must be -1 (disable) or at least 1, got {self.top_k}."
117	            )
118	        if not -2.0 <= self.frequency_penalty <= 2.0:
119	            raise ValueError(
120	                "frequency_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got "
121	                f"{self.frequency_penalty}."
122	            )
123	        if not -2.0 <= self.presence_penalty <= 2.0:
124	            raise ValueError(
125	                "presence_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got " f"{self.presence_penalty}."
126	            )
127	        if not 0.0 <= self.repetition_penalty <= 2.0:
128	            raise ValueError(
129	                "repetition_penalty must be in [0, 2], got "
130	                f"{self.repetition_penalty}."
131	            )
132	        if not 0 <= self.min_new_tokens:
133	            raise ValueError(
134	                f"min_new_tokens must be in [0, max_new_tokens], got "
135	                f"{self.min_new_tokens}."
136	            )
137	        if self.max_new_tokens is not None:
138	            if self.max_new_tokens < 0:
139	                raise ValueError(
140	                    f"max_new_tokens must be at least 0, got {self.max_new_tokens}."
141	                )
142	            if not self.min_new_tokens <= self.max_new_tokens:
143	                raise ValueError(
144	                    f"min_new_tokens must be in [0, max_new_tokens({self.max_new_tokens})], got "
145	                    f"{self.min_new_tokens}."
146	                )
147	        if self.logit_bias is not None:
148	            for token_id in self.logit_bias:
149	                if not 0 <= int(token_id) < vocab_size:
150	                    raise ValueError(
151	                        f"logit_bias must has keys in [0, {vocab_size - 1}], got "
152	                        f"{token_id}."
153	                    )
154	
155	        grammars = [
156	            self.json_schema,
157	            self.regex,
158	            self.ebnf,
159	        ]  # since mutually exclusive, only one can be set
160	        if sum(x is not None for x in grammars) > 1:
161	            raise ValueError("Only one of regex, json_schema, or ebnf can be set.")

163	    def normalize(self, tokenizer):
164	        # Process stop strings
165	        if self.stop_strs is None:
166	            self.stop_strs = []
167	            self.stop_str_max_len = 0
168	        else:

185	        else:
186	            if isinstance(self.stop_regex_strs, str):
187	                self.stop_regex_strs = [self.stop_regex_strs]
188	
189	            stop_regex_max_len = 0
190	            for stop_regex in self.stop_regex_strs:
191	                stop_regex_max_len = max(
192	                    stop_regex_max_len, get_max_seq_length(stop_regex)

194	
195	            self.stop_regex_max_len = stop_regex_max_len

198	# This function gets a strict upperbound on the maximum number of tokens that would need
199	# to be buffered to match the input regex string
200	# NOTE: in the worst case, one character that needs to be buffered corresponds to one
201	# token
202	def get_max_seq_length(regex_str: str):
203	    return _max_length_from_subpattern(sre_parse.parse(regex_str))
204	
205	
206	MAX_LEN = 2**30
207	
208	
209	def _max_length_from_subpattern(subpattern: sre_parse.SubPattern):
210	    total = 0
211	    for token, value in subpattern:
212	        if token in {
213	            sre_parse.LITERAL,  # `value` is any one character
214	            sre_parse.IN,  # Any character within `value`
215	            sre_parse.ANY,  # "."
216	        }:
217	            total += 1
218	        elif token == sre_parse.SUBPATTERN:
219	            # EG: (a\d+) ->
220	            # [(SUBPATTERN,
221	            #   (1, 0, 0, [(LITERAL, 97),
222	            #              (MAX_REPEAT, (1, MAXREPEAT, [(IN, [(CATEGORY, CATEGORY_DIGIT)])]))]))]
223	            _, _, _, inner_subpattern = value
224	            total += _max_length_from_subpattern(inner_subpattern)
225	        elif token == sre_parse.BRANCH:
226	            _, branches = value
227	            total += max(_max_length_from_subpattern(branch) for branch in branches)
228	        elif token in {sre_parse.MAX_REPEAT, sre_parse.MIN_REPEAT}:
229	            _, max_num_repeat, inner_subpattern = value
230	            if max_num_repeat == sre_parse.MAXREPEAT:
231	                total += MAX_LEN
232	            else:
233	                total += max_num_repeat * _max_length_from_subpattern(inner_subpattern)
234	        elif token == sre_parse.AT:
235	            # These are zero-width assertions like ^, $, and \b that don't add to the max
236	            # length
237	            total += 0
238	        else:
239	            logger.warning(f"Got unhandled regex token: {token}")
240	
241	            total += MAX_LEN
242	
243	    return total
244

67	    def process_req_with_grammar(self, req: Req) -> bool:
68	        # Init grammar cache for this request
69	        add_to_grammar_queue = False
70	        if (
71	            req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None
72	            or req.sampling_params.regex is not None
73	            or req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None
74	            or req.sampling_params.structural_tag is not None
75	        ):
76	            if self.grammar_backend is None:
77	                error_msg = "Grammar-based generation (json_schema, regex, ebnf, structural_tag) is not supported when the server is launched with --grammar-backend none"
78	                req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
79	            else:
80	                if req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None:
81	                    key = ("json", req.sampling_params.json_schema)
82	                elif req.sampling_params.regex is not None:
83	                    key = ("regex", req.sampling_params.regex)
84	                elif req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None:
85	                    key = ("ebnf", req.sampling_params.ebnf)
86	                elif req.sampling_params.structural_tag:
87	                    key = ("structural_tag", req.sampling_params.structural_tag)
88	
89	                value, cache_hit = self.grammar_backend.get_cached_or_future_value(
90	                    key, req.require_reasoning
91	                )
92	                req.grammar = value
93	
94	                if not cache_hit:
95	                    req.grammar_key = key
96	                    add_to_grammar_queue = True
97	                else:
98	                    if value is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:  # We hit a cached invalid grammar.
99	                        error_msg = f"Invalid grammar request with cache hit: {key=}"
100	                        req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
101	
102	        if add_to_grammar_queue:
103	            self.grammar_queue.append(req)
104	
105	        return add_to_grammar_queue
106	
107	    def get_ready_grammar_requests(self) -> List[Req]:
108	        """
109	        Move requests whose grammar objects are ready from grammar_queue to waiting_queue.
110	
111	        Rank i returns two sets ready_reqs_i, failed_reqs_i
112	        ready_reqs_all = all_gather(ready_reqs_i)
113	        failed_reqs_all = all_gather(failed_reqs_i)
114	
115	        ready_reqs = intersect(ready_reqs_all)
116	        failed_reqs = union(failed_reqs_all)

157	    def _init_value_dispatch(
158	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
159	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
160	        s = time.perf_counter()
161	        key_type, key_string = key
162	        if key_type == "json":
163	            grammar = self.dispatch_json(key_string)
164	        elif key_type == "regex":
165	            grammar = self.dispatch_regex(key_string)
166	        elif key_type == "ebnf":
167	            grammar = self.dispatch_ebnf(key_string)
168	        elif key_type == "structural_tag":
169	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)
170	        elif key_type == "structural_pattern":
171	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_pattern(key_string)
172	        elif key_type == "structural_pattern_v2":
173	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_pattern_v2(key_string)
174	        else:
175	            grammar = self.dispatch_fallback(key_type, key_string)
176	
177	        if grammar is not None and grammar.grammar_stats is not None:
178	            grammar.grammar_stats.compilation_time = time.perf_counter() - s
179	        return grammar

181	    def get_cached_or_future_value(
182	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
183	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
184	        value = self.cache.get(key)
185	        if value:
186	            copied_value = value.copy()
187	            copied_value.maybe_init_reasoning(require_reasoning)
188	            return copied_value, True
189	        value = self.executor.submit(self._init_value_dispatch, key, require_reasoning)
190	        return value, False

127	    def _handle_dtype_to_regex(self, sampling_params: SglSamplingParams):
128	        if sampling_params.dtype is None:
129	            return
130	
131	        if sampling_params.stop == ():
132	            sampling_params.stop = []
133	
134	        dtype_regex = None
135	        if sampling_params.dtype in ["int", int]:
136	
137	            dtype_regex = REGEX_INT
138	            sampling_params.stop.extend([" ", "\n"])
139	        elif sampling_params.dtype in ["float", float]:
140	
141	            dtype_regex = REGEX_FLOAT
142	            sampling_params.stop.extend([" ", "\n"])
143	        elif sampling_params.dtype in ["str", str]:
144	
145	            dtype_regex = REGEX_STR
146	        elif sampling_params.dtype in ["bool", bool]:
147	
148	            dtype_regex = REGEX_BOOL
149	        else:
150	            raise RuntimeError(f"Invalid dtype: {sampling_params.dtype}")
151	
152	        if dtype_regex is not None and sampling_params.regex is not None:
153	            warnings.warn(
154	                f"Both dtype and regex are set. Only dtype will be used. dtype: {sampling_params.dtype}, regex: {sampling_params.regex}"
155	            )
156	
157	        sampling_params.regex = dtype_regex
158	
159	    def generate(

164	        self._handle_dtype_to_regex(sampling_params)
165	        data = {
166	            "text": s.text_,
167	            "sampling_params": {
168	                "skip_special_tokens": global_config.skip_special_tokens_in_output,
169	                "spaces_between_special_tokens": global_config.spaces_between_special_tokens_in_out,
170	                **sampling_params.to_srt_kwargs(),
171	            },
172	        }
173	
174	        for item in [
175	            "return_logprob",
176	            "logprob_start_len",
177	            "top_logprobs_num",
178	            "return_text_in_logprobs",
179	        ]:

## Comprehensive SGLang Output Constraints Architecture Report

Based on my exploration of the SGLang codebase, here's a detailed analysis of how output constraints are structured and implemented:

### 1. **Constraint Architecture Overview**

The constraint system is organized into three main layers:

#### **Frontend DSL Layer** (`python/sglang/lang/`)
- **File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493191991-5su1ov/python/sglang/lang/api.py`
  - Lines 75-139: The `gen()` function supports constraint parameters:
    - `dtype`: Optional[Union[type, str]] - for type-constrained generation (int, float, bool, str)
    - `choices`: Optional[List[str]] - for choice constraints
    - `regex`: Optional[str] - for regex-constrained output
    - `json_schema`: Optional[str] - for JSON schema-constrained output

#### **Intermediate Representation (IR) Layer** (`python/sglang/lang/ir.py`)
- **File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493191991-5su1ov/python/sglang/lang/ir.py`
  - Lines 17-41: `SglSamplingParams` dataclass stores constraints:
    ```python
    json_schema: Optional[str] = None
    dtype: Optional[str] = None
    regex: Optional[str] = None
    ```
  - Lines 11-14: Pre-defined regex patterns for dtype constraints:
    - `REGEX_INT = r"[-+]?[0-9]+[ \n]*"`
    - `REGEX_FLOAT = r"[-+]?[0-9]*\.?[0-9]+[ \n]*"`
    - `REGEX_BOOL = r"(True|False)"`
    - `REGEX_STR = r"\"[\w\d\s]*\""`

---

### 2. **Runtime Sampling Parameters**

**File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493191991-5su1ov/python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py`

The `SamplingParams` class (lines 31-96) is the core runtime parameter container:

```python
class SamplingParams:
    def __init__(
        self,
        ...
        json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
        regex: Optional[str] = None,
        ebnf: Optional[str] = None,
        structural_tag: Optional[str] = None,
        ...
    ):
```

**Constraint Types Supported**:
- `json_schema`: JSON Schema string for JSON validation (lines 85)
- `regex`: Regular expression pattern (line 83)
- `ebnf`: Extended Backus-Naur Form grammar (line 86)
- `structural_tag`: Structural tag constraint (line 87)

**Validation** (lines 155-161):
```python
grammars = [self.json_schema, self.regex, self.ebnf]
if sum(x is not None for x in grammars) > 1:
    raise ValueError("Only one of regex, json_schema, or ebnf can be set.")
```
Only ONE constraint can be active at a time.

---

### 3. **Constraint Backend Architecture**

#### **Base Grammar Backend** (Abstract Factory Pattern)
**File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493191991-5su1ov/python/sglang/srt/constrained/base_grammar_backend.py`

**Key Classes**:

1. **`BaseGrammarObject`** (lines 42-117):
   - Abstract base class for all grammar constraints
   - Key methods:
     - `accept_token(token: int)`: Accept a token in the grammar
     - `fill_vocab_mask(vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int)`: Constrain vocabulary at generation time
     - `allocate_vocab_mask()`, `apply_vocab_mask()`: Tensor operations for efficient masking
     - `try_jump_forward()`, `jump_forward_str_state()`: Performance optimization for long literals
     - `copy()`: Create independent copy for concurrent requests

2. **`BaseGrammarBackend`** (lines 129-196):
   - Registry and dispatcher for constraint types
   - Dispatch methods (method pattern):
     ```python
     def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]
     def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]
     def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]
     def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]
     ```
   - Caching mechanism with thread pool executor (lines 189-190)

3. **`GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY`** (line 199):
   - Global registry for custom constraint backends
   - Registration function: `register_grammar_backend(name, init_func)`

#### **Grammar Manager** (Request Processing)
**File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493191991-5su1ov/python/sglang/srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py`

**Key Method**: `process_req_with_grammar()` (lines 67-105):
- Checks if request has constraints (json_schema, regex, ebnf, structural_tag)
- Creates cache key: `key = ("json", schema)`, `("regex", pattern)`, etc.
- Lazily compiles constraints in background (lines 89-90)
- Returns bool indicating if request was queued for grammar compilation

---

### 4. **Concrete Backend Implementations**

#### **A. Outlines Backend**
**File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493191991-5su1ov/python/sglang/srt/constrained/outlines_backend.py`

**Implementation Details**:
- `OutlinesGrammar` (lines 42-112): Wraps `outlines.fsm.guide.RegexGuide`
- `OutlinesGrammarBackend` (lines 114-178):
  - `dispatch_json()` (lines 166-175):
    - Converts JSON schema to regex using `build_regex_from_schema()`
    - Delegates to `_compile_regex()`
  - `dispatch_regex()` (lines 177-178): Direct regex compilation
  - Private method `_compile_regex()` (lines 145-158): Creates RegexGuide object

**Key Function**: `build_regex_from_object()` (lines 181-190):
```python
def build_regex_from_object(
    object: Union[str, BaseModel, Dict], 
    whitespace_pattern: Optional[str] = None
):

    # then calls outlines' build_regex_from_schema()
```

#### **B. XGrammar Backend**
**File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493191991-5su1ov/python/sglang/srt/constrained/xgrammar_backend.py`

**Implementation Details**:

- `XGrammarGrammarBackend` (lines 175+):
  - Inherits from `BaseGrammarBackend`
  - Implements bitmask-based token filtering (more efficient than masking)
  - Constructor creates `GrammarCompiler` from tokenizer

---

### 5. **Constraint Registration & Dispatch Flow**

**Registration Function**: `create_grammar_backend()` (lines 206-274 of base_grammar_backend.py)

```python
def create_grammar_backend(
    server_args: ServerArgs,
    tokenizer,
    vocab_size: int,
    eos_token_ids: Optional[set] = None,
) -> Optional[BaseGrammarBackend]:
    name = server_args.grammar_backend
    
    # Priority 1: Check custom registry
    if name in GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY:
        return GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name](...)
    
    # Priority 2: Built-in backends
    if name == "outlines":
        return OutlinesGrammarBackend(...)
    elif name == "xgrammar":
        return XGrammarGrammarBackend(...)
    elif name == "llguidance":
        return GuidanceBackend(...)
    elif name == "none":
        return None
```

**Server Args Configuration**:
**File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493191991-5su1ov/python/sglang/srt/server_args.py`
- Line 453: `grammar_backend: Optional[str] = None`
- Line 2063-2065: `_handle_grammar_backend()` auto-detects (defaults to "xgrammar")

---

### 6. **How dtype Constraints Are Handled (Special Case)**

**File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493191991-5su1ov/python/sglang/lang/backend/runtime_endpoint.py`

**Key Method**: `_handle_dtype_to_regex()` (lines 127-157):
```python
def _handle_dtype_to_regex(self, sampling_params: SglSamplingParams):
    if sampling_params.dtype is None:
        return
    
    # dtype is converted to regex constraint
    if sampling_params.dtype in ["int", int]:
        sampling_params.regex = REGEX_INT
        sampling_params.stop.extend([" ", "\n"])
    elif sampling_params.dtype in ["float", float]:
        sampling_params.regex = REGEX_FLOAT
        sampling_params.stop.extend([" ", "\n"])
    # ... etc
```

**Important**: `dtype` is NOT a separate constraint type; it's converted to `regex` before sending to backend.

---

### 7. **Usage in DSL Programs**

**File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493191991-5su1ov/test/kits/json_constrained_kit.py`

**Example Usage**:
```python
response = requests.post(
    base_url + "/generate",
    json={
        "text": "Generate a JSON object...",
        "sampling_params": {
            "temperature": 0,
            "max_new_tokens": 128,
            "json_schema": json_schema_dict,  # JSON Schema as dict
        },
    },
)
```

**Test Files**:
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493191991-5su1ov/test/registered/constrained_decoding/test_constrained_decoding.py`
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493191991-5su1ov/test/registered/function_call/test_json_schema_constraint.py`

---

### 8. **Function Call / Tool Choice Constraint** (JSON Path Precursor)

**File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493191991-5su1ov/python/sglang/srt/function_call/utils.py`

**Key Function**: `get_json_schema_constraint()` (lines 207-246):
- Generates JSON schema constraints for OpenAI-style tool calls
- Accepts tool definitions and returns JSON schema as constraint
- Example: Lines 232-244 show how array-of-objects schema is created for multiple tool choices

This demonstrates a pattern for **derived constraints**: starting with structured input (tools), generating a JSON schema constraint that the grammar backend can enforce.

---

### 9. **Class Hierarchy Summary**

```
BaseGrammarObject (Abstract)
├── OutlinesGrammar (wraps outlines.fsm.guide.RegexGuide)
├── XGrammarGrammar (wraps xgrammar.GrammarMatcher)
└── [Other backend implementations]

BaseGrammarBackend (Abstract Dispatcher/Factory)
├── OutlinesGrammarBackend
├── XGrammarGrammarBackend
├── GuidanceBackend (llguidance)
└── ReasonerGrammarBackend (wrapper for reasoning models)
```

---

### 10. **How to Add a New "json_path" Constraint**

Based on the architecture, here are the steps:

1. **Add parameter to SglSamplingParams** (`python/sglang/lang/ir.py`, line ~40):
   ```python
   json_path: Optional[str] = None
   ```

2. **Add parameter to gen() DSL function** (`python/sglang/lang/api.py`, line ~98):
   ```python
   json_path: Optional[str] = None
   ```

3. **Add to SamplingParams** (`python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py`):
   ```python
   self.json_path = json_path
   ```
   And update validation (line 160) to check mutual exclusivity with other constraints.

4. **Add constraint type dispatch** (`python/sglang/srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py`, lines 80-87):
   ```python
   elif req.sampling_params.json_path is not None:
       key = ("json_path", req.sampling_params.json_path)
   ```

5. **Implement dispatch method in backend** (`base_grammar_backend.py`, line ~154):
   ```python
   def dispatch_json_path(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
       return self._not_supported("json_path", key_string)
   ```

6. **Add dispatch case** (`base_grammar_backend.py`, line ~170):
   ```python
   elif key_type == "json_path":
       grammar = self.dispatch_json_path(key_string)
   ```

7. **Implement in concrete backends** (e.g., `outlines_backend.py`):
   ```python
   def dispatch_json_path(self, key_string: str):
       # Parse json_path from key_string
       # Build grammar that constrains output to valid JSON subpaths
       regex = build_regex_from_json_path(key_string)
       return self._compile_regex(regex)
   ```

8. **Pass through interpreter** (`python/sglang/lang/interpreter.py`, line ~809):
   - Add "json_path" to the list of constraint parameters copied from sampling_params

---

### **Key Files Summary for Implementation**

| Layer | File Path | Purpose |
|-------|-----------|---------|
| **DSL** | `python/sglang/lang/api.py` | `gen()` function parameters |
| **IR** | `python/sglang/lang/ir.py` | `SglSamplingParams` dataclass |
| **Runtime Params** | `python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py` | `SamplingParams` class |
| **Manager** | `python/sglang/srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py` | Request constraint processing |
| **Base Architecture** | `python/sglang/srt/constrained/base_grammar_backend.py` | Base classes & registry |
| **Outlines Backend** | `python/sglang/srt/constrained/outlines_backend.py` | Regex-based implementation |
| **XGrammar Backend** | `python/sglang/srt/constrained/xgrammar_backend.py` | Bitmask-based implementation |
| **Server Args** | `python/sglang/srt/server_args.py` | Grammar backend configuration |
| **Interpreter** | `python/sglang/lang/interpreter.py` | Parameter forwarding |
| **Tests** | `test/registered/constrained_decoding/test_constrained_decoding.py` | Test patterns |

---

This architecture demonstrates a clean **separation of concerns** with a **plugin-based backend system**. The constraint system is designed to be extensible while maintaining performance through caching and lazy compilation. The `json_path` constraint would follow the same pattern as existing constraints, building on the established JSON schema handling infrastructure.

**Key Method**: `_handle_dtype_to_regex()` (lines 127-157):
```python
def _handle_dtype_to_regex(self, sampling_params: SglSamplingParams):
    if sampling_params.dtype is None:
        return
    
    # dtype is converted to regex constraint
    if sampling_params.dtype in ["int", int]:
        sampling_params.regex = REGEX_INT
        sampling_params.stop.extend([" ", "\n"])
    elif sampling_params.dtype in ["float", float]:
        sampling_params.regex = REGEX_FLOAT
        sampling_params.stop.extend([" ", "\n"])
    # ... etc
```

**Important**: `dtype` is NOT a separate constraint type; it's converted to `regex` before sending to backend.

## 4. BASEGRAMMARBACKEND DISPATCH PATTERN

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493191991-5su1ov/python/sglang/srt/constrained/base_grammar_backend.py:129-179`

### Dispatch Methods Template:

```python
class BaseGrammarBackend:
    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
        return self._not_supported("json", key_string)
    
    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
        return self._not_supported("regex", key_string)
    
    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
        return self._not_supported("ebnf", key_string)
    
    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
        return self._not_supported("structural_tag", key_string)
    
    def _init_value_dispatch(
        self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
        s = time.perf_counter()
        key_type, key_string = key
        
        # Dispatch based on key_type
        if key_type == "json":
            grammar = self.dispatch_json(key_string)
        elif key_type == "regex":
            grammar = self.dispatch_regex(key_string)
        elif key_type == "ebnf":
            grammar = self.dispatch_ebnf(key_string)
        elif key_type == "structural_tag":
            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)
        # ... additional dispatch cases ...
        else:
            grammar = self.dispatch_fallback(key_type, key_string)
        
        # Compilation timing tracked
        if grammar is not None and grammar.grammar_stats is not None:
            grammar.grammar_stats.compilation_time = time.perf_counter() - s
        return grammar
```

## 5. HOW CONSTRAINTS REACH THE MANAGER

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493191991-5su1ov/python/sglang/srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py:67-105`

```python
def process_req_with_grammar(self, req: Req) -> bool:
    if (
        req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None
        or req.sampling_params.regex is not None
        or req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None
        or req.sampling_params.structural_tag is not None
    ):
        # Determine which constraint is set
        if req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None:
            key = ("json", req.sampling_params.json_schema)
        elif req.sampling_params.regex is not None:
            key = ("regex", req.sampling_params.regex)
        elif req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None:
            key = ("ebnf", req.sampling_params.ebnf)
        elif req.sampling_params.structural_tag:
            key = ("structural_tag", req.sampling_params.structural_tag)
        
        # Cache lookup and dispatch
        value, cache_hit = self.grammar_backend.get_cached_or_future_value(
            key, req.require_reasoning
        )
```

### OutlinesBackend Example
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493191991-5su1ov/python/sglang/srt/constrained/outlines_backend.py:114-178`

```python
class OutlinesGrammarBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
    def __init__(self, tokenizer, whitespace_pattern: str | None):
        # Initialize with tokenizer and whitespace pattern
        self.outlines_tokenizer = TransformerTokenizer(tokenizer)
        self.whitespace_pattern = whitespace_pattern
    
    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[OutlinesGrammar]:
        try:
            regex = build_regex_from_object(
                key_string,
                whitespace_pattern=self.whitespace_pattern,
            )
        except (NotImplementedError, json.decoder.JSONDecodeError, ValueError) as e:
            logger.error(f"Hit invalid json_schema: {key_string=}, {e=}")
            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
        return self._compile_regex(regex)
    
    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str):
        return self._compile_regex(key_string)
    
    def _compile_regex(self, regex: str) -> Optional[OutlinesGrammar]:
        try:
            guide = RegexGuide.from_regex(regex, self.outlines_tokenizer)
        except interegular.patterns.InvalidSyntax as e:
            logger.error(f"Hit invalid regex schema: {regex=}, {e=}")
            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
        return OutlinesGrammar(guide, jump_forward_map=None)
```

### XGrammarBackend Example
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493191991-5su1ov/python/sglang/srt/constrained/xgrammar_backend.py:175-287`

```python
class XGrammarGrammarBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
    def __init__(self, tokenizer, vocab_size: int, model_eos_token_ids=None, any_whitespace=True):
        self.grammar_compiler = GrammarCompiler(tokenizer_info=tokenizer_info)
        self.vocab_size = vocab_size
    
    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
        try:
            ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_json_schema(
                schema=key_string, any_whitespace=self.any_whitespace
            )
        except (RuntimeError, json.decoder.JSONDecodeError, UnicodeDecodeError) as e:
            logger.error(f"Hit invalid json_schema: {key_string=}, {e=}")
            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
        return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="json"))
    
    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
        try:
            ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_grammar(key_string)
        except RuntimeError as e:
            logger.error(f"Hit invalid ebnf: {key_string=}, {e=}")
            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
        return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="ebnf"))
    
    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
        try:
            ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_regex(key_string)
        except RuntimeError as e:
            logger.error(f"Hit invalid regex: {key_string=}, {e=}")
            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
        return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="regex"))
```

## 9. JSON SCHEMA CONSTRAINT IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS

### JSON Schema Constraint Handler
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493191991-5su1ov/python/sglang/srt/function_call/utils.py:207-246`

```python
def get_json_schema_constraint(
    tools: List[Tool], tool_choice: Union[ToolChoice, Literal["required"]]
) -> Optional[dict]:
    """Get the JSON schema constraint for the specified tool choice."""
    
    if isinstance(tool_choice, ToolChoice):
        # For specific function choice
        fn_name = tool_choice.function.name
        for tool in tools:
            if tool.function.name == fn_name:
                return {
                    "type": "array",
                    "minItems": 1,
                    "maxItems": 1,
                    "items": _get_tool_schema(tool),
                }
        return None
    elif tool_choice == "required":
        json_schema = {
            "type": "array",
            "minItems": 1,
            "items": {
                "type": "object",
                "anyOf": [_get_tool_schema(tool) for tool in tools],
            },
        }
        json_schema_defs = _get_tool_schema_defs(tools)
        if json_schema_defs:
            json_schema["$defs"] = json_schema_defs
        return json_schema
    
    return None
```

## 11. KEY VALIDATION PATTERNS USED

1. **Mutual Exclusivity:** Only one constraint type per request (SamplingParams.verify)
2. **Early Validation:** Regex validated in api.py before SglGen creation

5. **Error Handling:** INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ returned for compilation failures

## Summary Report: SGLang Constraint System Architecture

Based on my thorough exploration of the SGLang codebase, here's a complete guide for implementing a new "json_path" output constraint:

---

## 1. EXISTING CONSTRAINT TYPES

SGLang currently supports the following constraint types (mutually exclusive):

| Type | Key Type | Storage Location | Validation |
|------|----------|-----------------|-----------|
| **json_schema** | `"json"` | `SamplingParams.json_schema` | JSON Schema string |
| **regex** | `"regex"` | `SamplingParams.regex` | Regex pattern string |
| **ebnf** | `"ebnf"` | `SamplingParams.ebnf` | EBNF grammar string |
| **structural_tag** | `"structural_tag"` | `SamplingParams.structural_tag` | Xgrammar StructuralTag format |

The constraint validation logic is in:
- **File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493191991-5su1ov/python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py` (lines 155-161)
- **Constraint:** Only ONE of {regex, json_schema, ebnf, structural_tag} can be set simultaneously

---

## 2. CONSTRAINT FLOW ARCHITECTURE

```
[Backend receives constraint via API]

    ↓
```

## 4. BASEGRAMMARBACKEND DISPATCH PATTERN

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493191991-5su1ov/python/sglang/srt/constrained/base_grammar_backend.py:129-179`

### Dispatch Methods Template:

```python
class BaseGrammarBackend:
    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
        return self._not_supported("json", key_string)
    
    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
        return self._not_supported("regex", key_string)
    
    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
        return self._not_supported("ebnf", key_string)
    
    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
        return self._not_supported("structural_tag", key_string)
    
    def _init_value_dispatch(
        self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
        s = time.perf_counter()
        key_type, key_string = key
        
        # Dispatch based on key_type
        if key_type == "json":
            grammar = self.dispatch_json(key_string)
        elif key_type == "regex":
            grammar = self.dispatch_regex(key_string)
        elif key_type == "ebnf":
            grammar = self.dispatch_ebnf(key_string)
        elif key_type == "structural_tag":
            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)
        # ... additional dispatch cases ...
        else:
            grammar = self.dispatch_fallback(key_type, key_string)
        
        # Compilation timing tracked
        if grammar is not None and grammar.grammar_stats is not None:
            grammar.grammar_stats.compilation_time = time.perf_counter() - s
        return grammar
```

---

## 5. HOW CONSTRAINTS REACH THE MANAGER

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493191991-5su1ov/python/sglang/srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py:67-105`

```python
def process_req_with_grammar(self, req: Req) -> bool:
    if (
        req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None
        or req.sampling_params.regex is not None
        or req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None
        or req.sampling_params.structural_tag is not None
    ):
        # Determine which constraint is set
        if req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None:
            key = ("json", req.sampling_params.json_schema)
        elif req.sampling_params.regex is not None:
            key = ("regex", req.sampling_params.regex)
        elif req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None:
            key = ("ebnf", req.sampling_params.ebnf)
        elif req.sampling_params.structural_tag:
            key = ("structural_tag", req.sampling_params.structural_tag)
        
        # Cache lookup and dispatch
        value, cache_hit = self.grammar_backend.get_cached_or_future_value(
            key, req.require_reasoning
        )
```

## 7. CONSTRAINT IMPLEMENTATION EXAMPLES

### OutlinesBackend Example
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493191991-5su1ov/python/sglang/srt/constrained/outlines_backend.py:114-178`

```python
class OutlinesGrammarBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
    def __init__(self, tokenizer, whitespace_pattern: str | None):
        # Initialize with tokenizer and whitespace pattern
        self.outlines_tokenizer = TransformerTokenizer(tokenizer)
        self.whitespace_pattern = whitespace_pattern
    
    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[OutlinesGrammar]:
        try:
            regex = build_regex_from_object(
                key_string,
                whitespace_pattern=self.whitespace_pattern,
            )
        except (NotImplementedError, json.decoder.JSONDecodeError, ValueError) as e:
            logger.error(f"Hit invalid json_schema: {key_string=}, {e=}")
            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
        return self._compile_regex(regex)
    
    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str):
        return self._compile_regex(key_string)
    
    def _compile_regex(self, regex: str) -> Optional[OutlinesGrammar]:
        try:
            guide = RegexGuide.from_regex(regex, self.outlines_tokenizer)
        except interegular.patterns.InvalidSyntax as e:
            logger.error(f"Hit invalid regex schema: {regex=}, {e=}")
            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
        return OutlinesGrammar(guide, jump_forward_map=None)
```

### XGrammarBackend Example
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493191991-5su1ov/python/sglang/srt/constrained/xgrammar_backend.py:175-287`

```python
class XGrammarGrammarBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
    def __init__(self, tokenizer, vocab_size: int, model_eos_token_ids=None, any_whitespace=True):
        self.grammar_compiler = GrammarCompiler(tokenizer_info=tokenizer_info)
        self.vocab_size = vocab_size
    
    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
        try:
            ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_json_schema(
                schema=key_string, any_whitespace=self.any_whitespace
            )
        except (RuntimeError, json.decoder.JSONDecodeError, UnicodeDecodeError) as e:
            logger.error(f"Hit invalid json_schema: {key_string=}, {e=}")
            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
        return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="json"))
    
    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
        try:
            ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_grammar(key_string)
        except RuntimeError as e:
            logger.error(f"Hit invalid ebnf: {key_string=}, {e=}")
            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
        return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="ebnf"))
    
    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
        try:
            ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_regex(key_string)
        except RuntimeError as e:
            logger.error(f"Hit invalid regex: {key_string=}, {e=}")
            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
        return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="regex"))
```

### Constraint Validation Function
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493191991-5su1ov/python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py:155-161`

```python
    grammars = [
        self.json_schema,
        self.regex,
        self.ebnf,
    ]
    if sum(x is not None for x in grammars) > 1:
        raise ValueError("Only one of regex, json_schema, or ebnf can be set.")
```

## 9. JSON SCHEMA CONSTRAINT IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS

### JSON Schema Constraint Handler
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493191991-5su1ov/python/sglang/srt/function_call/utils.py:207-246`

```python
def get_json_schema_constraint(
    tools: List[Tool], tool_choice: Union[ToolChoice, Literal["required"]]
) -> Optional[dict]:
    """Get the JSON schema constraint for the specified tool choice."""
    
    if isinstance(tool_choice, ToolChoice):
        # For specific function choice
        fn_name = tool_choice.function.name
        for tool in tools:
            if tool.function.name == fn_name:
                return {
                    "type": "array",
                    "minItems": 1,
                    "maxItems": 1,
                    "items": _get_tool_schema(tool),
                }
        return None
    elif tool_choice == "required":
        json_schema = {
            "type": "array",
            "minItems": 1,
            "items": {
                "type": "object",
                "anyOf": [_get_tool_schema(tool) for tool in tools],
            },
        }
        json_schema_defs = _get_tool_schema_defs(tools)
        if json_schema_defs:
            json_schema["$defs"] = json_schema_defs
        return json_schema
    
    return None
```

### 6. **Update BaseGrammarBackend** (srt/constrained/base_grammar_backend.py)
   - Add dispatch method: `def dispatch_json_path(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]`
   - Add case in `_init_value_dispatch()`:


### 7. **Implement in OutlinesBackend** (srt/constrained/outlines_backend.py)

87	                    key = ("structural_tag", req.sampling_params.structural_tag)
88	
89	                value, cache_hit = self.grammar_backend.get_cached_or_future_value(
90	                    key, req.require_reasoning
91	                )
92	                req.grammar = value
93	
94	                if not cache_hit:
95	                    req.grammar_key = key
96	                    add_to_grammar_queue = True
97	                else:
98	                    if value is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:  # We hit a cached invalid grammar.
99	                        error_msg = f"Invalid grammar request with cache hit: {key=}"
100	                        req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
101	
102	        if add_to_grammar_queue:
103	            self.grammar_queue.append(req)
104	
105	        return add_to_grammar_queue
106	
107	    def get_ready_grammar_requests(self) -> List[Req]:
108	        """
109	        Move requests whose grammar objects are ready from grammar_queue to waiting_queue.
110	
111	        Rank i returns two sets ready_reqs_i, failed_reqs_i
112	        ready_reqs_all = all_gather(ready_reqs_i)
113	        failed_reqs_all = all_gather(failed_reqs_i)
114	
115	        ready_reqs = intersect(ready_reqs_all)
116	        failed_reqs = union(failed_reqs_all)
117	        """
118	        ready_req_idxs: set[int] = set()
119	        failed_req_idxs: set[int] = set()
120	
121	        # Poll for ready requests
122	        start_time = time.perf_counter()
123	        while time.perf_counter() - start_time < self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL:
124	            for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue):
125	                if i in ready_req_idxs:
126	                    continue
127	
128	                if req.finished() or req.grammar is None:  # It is aborted by AbortReq
129	                    ready_req_idxs.add(i)
130	                    continue
131	
132	                assert isinstance(req.grammar, futures.Future), f"{req=}"
133	                if req.grammar.done():
134	                    ready_req_idxs.add(i)
135	
136	            # Sleep a bit to avoid busy waiting
137	            time.sleep(self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL / 10)
138	
139	        # Check failed requests
140	        for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue):
141	            if i not in ready_req_idxs:
142	                self.grammar_queue[i].grammar_wait_ct += 1
143	                if (
144	                    self.grammar_queue[i].grammar_wait_ct
145	                    >= self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS
146	                ):
147	                    # Timeout after max poll iterations
148	                    # The actual waiting time is SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS * max(SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL, GPU_forward_batch_latency)
149	                    failed_req_idxs.add(i)
150	
151	        # Sync ready and failed requests across all ranks
152	        if self.grammar_sync_size == 1:
153	            synced_ready_req_idxs = ready_req_idxs
154	            synced_failed_req_idxs = failed_req_idxs
155	        else:
156	            all_gather_output = [None] * self.grammar_sync_size
157	            torch.distributed.all_gather_object(
158	                all_gather_output,
159	                (ready_req_idxs, failed_req_idxs),
160	                group=self.grammar_sync_group,
161	            )
162	            synced_ready_req_idxs = set.intersection(*[x[0] for x in all_gather_output])
163	            synced_failed_req_idxs = set.union(*[x[1] for x in all_gather_output])
164	
165	        # Return ready requests
166	        return_reqs: List[Req] = []
167	        for i in synced_ready_req_idxs:
168	            req = self.grammar_queue[i]
169	            return_reqs.append(req)
170	            if req.finished() or req.grammar is None:  # It is aborted by AbortReq
171	                continue
172	
173	            req.grammar = req.grammar.result()
174	            self.grammar_backend.set_cache(req.grammar_key, req.grammar.copy())
175	            if req.grammar is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:
176	                error_msg = f"Invalid grammar request: {req.grammar_key=}"
177	                req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
178	
179	        # Return failed requests
180	        for i in synced_failed_req_idxs:
181	            req = self.grammar_queue[i]
182	            return_reqs.append(req)
183	
184	            req.grammar.cancel()
185	            self.grammar_backend.set_cache(req.grammar_key, INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ)
186	            error_msg = f"Grammar preprocessing timed out: {req.grammar_key=}"
187	            req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
188	
189	        # Remove finished requests from grammar_queue
190	        self.grammar_queue = [
191	            req
192	            for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue)
193	            if i not in synced_ready_req_idxs and i not in synced_failed_req_idxs
194	        ]
195	        return return_reqs
