14	"""Constrained decoding with xgrammar backend."""
15	
16	import dataclasses
17	import json
18	import logging
19	from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Tuple, Union
20	
21	import torch
22	from xgrammar import (
23	    CompiledGrammar,
24	    GrammarCompiler,
25	    GrammarMatcher,
26	    StructuralTagItem,
27	    TokenizerInfo,
28	    allocate_token_bitmask,
29	)
30	
31	from sglang.srt.constrained.base_grammar_backend import (
32	    INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ,
33	    BaseGrammarBackend,
34	    BaseGrammarObject,
35	    GrammarStats,
36	)
37	from sglang.srt.constrained.utils import is_legacy_structural_tag
38	from sglang.srt.utils import is_hip
39	
40	_is_hip = is_hip()
41	if _is_hip:
42	    from sgl_kernel import apply_token_bitmask_inplace_cuda
43	else:
44	    from sglang.srt.constrained.triton_ops.bitmask_ops import (
45	        apply_token_bitmask_inplace_triton,
46	    )
47	
48	
49	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
50	MAX_ROLLBACK_TOKENS = 200
51	
52	
53	class XGrammarGrammar(BaseGrammarObject):
54	
55	    def __init__(
56	        self,
57	        matcher: GrammarMatcher,
58	        vocab_size: int,
59	        ctx: CompiledGrammar,
60	        override_stop_tokens: Optional[Union[List[int], int]],
61	        key_string: Optional[str] = None,  # TODO (sk): for debugging, remove later
62	        grammar_stats: Optional[GrammarStats] = GrammarStats(),
63	    ) -> None:
64	        super().__init__()
65	        self.matcher = matcher
66	        self.vocab_size = vocab_size
67	        self.ctx = ctx
68	        self.override_stop_tokens = override_stop_tokens
69	        self.accepted_tokens = []
70	        self.key_string = key_string
71	        self.grammar_stats = grammar_stats
72	
73	    def accept_token(self, token: int):
74	        if not self.is_terminated():
75	            self.current_token = token
76	            accepted = self.matcher.accept_token(token)
77	            if not accepted:
78	                # log for debugging
79	                raise ValueError(
80	                    f"Tokens not accepted: {token}\n"
81	                    f"Accepted tokens: {self.accepted_tokens}\n"
82	                    f"Key string: {self.key_string}"
83	                )
84	            else:
85	                self.accepted_tokens.append(token)
86	
87	    def rollback(self, k: int):
88	        self.matcher.rollback(k)
89	        self.accepted_tokens = self.accepted_tokens[:-k]
90	
91	    def is_terminated(self):
92	        return self.matcher.is_terminated()
93	
94	    def allocate_vocab_mask(
95	        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
96	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
97	        return allocate_token_bitmask(batch_size, vocab_size)
98	
99	    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
100	        self.matcher.fill_next_token_bitmask(vocab_mask, idx)
101	
102	    @staticmethod
103	    def move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor:
104	        return vocab_mask.to(device, non_blocking=True)
105	
106	    def apply_vocab_mask(self, logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor) -> None:
107	        if (
108	            logits.device.type == "cuda"
109	            or logits.device.type == "npu"
110	            or logits.device.type == "xpu"
111	        ):
112	            if _is_hip:
113	                apply_token_bitmask_inplace_cuda(logits, vocab_mask)
114	            else:
115	                apply_token_bitmask_inplace_triton(logits, vocab_mask)
116	        elif logits.device.type == "cpu" and self.apply_vocab_mask_cpu:
117	            self.apply_vocab_mask_cpu(logits, vocab_mask)
118	        else:
119	            raise RuntimeError(f"Unsupported device: {logits.device.type}")
120	
121	    def copy(self):
122	        matcher = GrammarMatcher(
123	            self.ctx,
124	            max_rollback_tokens=MAX_ROLLBACK_TOKENS,
125	            override_stop_tokens=self.override_stop_tokens,
126	        )
127	        return XGrammarGrammar(
128	            matcher,
129	            self.vocab_size,
130	            self.ctx,
131	            self.override_stop_tokens,
132	            self.key_string,
133	            dataclasses.replace(
134	                self.grammar_stats, is_cache_hit=True, tree_traversal_time=[]
135	            ),
136	        )
137	
138	    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer) -> Optional[Tuple[List[int], str]]:
139	        s = self.matcher.find_jump_forward_string()
140	        if s:
141	            return [], s
142	        return None
143	
144	    def jump_forward_str_state(self, helper: Tuple[List[int], str]) -> Tuple[str, int]:
145	        _, data = helper
146	        return data, -1
147	
148	    def jump_and_retokenize(
149	        self, old_output_ids: List[int], new_output_ids: List[int], next_state: int
150	    ):
151	        k = 0
152	        for i, old_id in enumerate(old_output_ids):
153	            if old_id == new_output_ids[i]:
154	                k = i + 1
155	            else:
156	                break
157	
158	        # rollback to the last token that is the same
159	        if k < len(old_output_ids):
160	            self.matcher.rollback(len(old_output_ids) - k)
161	
162	        for i in range(k, len(new_output_ids)):
163	            assert self.matcher.accept_token(new_output_ids[i])
164	
165	    def __repr__(self):
166	        return f"XGrammarGrammar({self.key_string=}, {self.accepted_tokens=}, {self.current_token=})"
167	
168	
169	class TokenizerNotSupportedError(Exception):
170	    """Raised when tokenizer is not supported by XGrammar backend."""
171	
172	    pass
173	
174	
175	class XGrammarGrammarBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
176	    def __init__(
177	        self,
178	        tokenizer,
179	        vocab_size: int,
180	        model_eos_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
181	        any_whitespace: bool = True,
182	    ):
183	        super().__init__()
184	
185	        if hasattr(tokenizer, "init_xgrammar"):
186	            # For special tokenizer
187	            tokenizer_info, override_stop_tokens = tokenizer.init_xgrammar()
188	
189	            if tokenizer_info is None:
190	                # Not supported tokenizer
191	                raise TokenizerNotSupportedError(
192	                    f"Tokenizer type {type(tokenizer).__name__} is not supported by XGrammar"
193	                )
194	        else:
195	            # Create TokenizerInfo with model's EOS tokens as the authoritative stop tokens
196	            # This ensures consistency between what the model considers EOS and what XGrammar uses
197	            try:
198	                tokenizer_info = TokenizerInfo.from_huggingface(
199	                    tokenizer, vocab_size=vocab_size, stop_token_ids=model_eos_token_ids
200	                )
201	                override_stop_tokens = None
202	            except Exception as e:
203	                raise TokenizerNotSupportedError(
204	                    f"Failed to create XGrammar TokenizerInfo from tokenizer: {e}"
205	                )
206	
207	        self.grammar_compiler = GrammarCompiler(tokenizer_info=tokenizer_info)
208	        self.vocab_size = vocab_size
209	        self.override_stop_tokens = override_stop_tokens
210	        self.any_whitespace = any_whitespace
211	
212	    @staticmethod
213	    def _sanitize_structural_format(structural_format):
214	        """Recursively replace missing json_schema fields with an empty schema."""
215	        if not isinstance(structural_format, dict):
216	            return
217	
218	        fmt_type = structural_format.get("type")
219	        if fmt_type in {"json_schema", "qwen_xml_parameter"}:
220	            if structural_format.get("json_schema") is None:
221	                structural_format["json_schema"] = {}
222	
223	        if fmt_type == "tag":
224	            XGrammarGrammarBackend._sanitize_structural_format(
225	                structural_format.get("content")
226	            )
227	        elif fmt_type in {"sequence", "or"}:
228	            for element in structural_format.get("elements", []):
229	                XGrammarGrammarBackend._sanitize_structural_format(element)
230	        elif fmt_type in {"triggered_tags", "tags_with_separator"}:
231	            for tag in structural_format.get("tags", []):
232	                XGrammarGrammarBackend._sanitize_structural_format(tag)
233	
234	    @staticmethod
235	    def _sanitize_structural_tag_structures(structural_tag: Dict) -> None:
236	        for structure in structural_tag.get("structures", []):
237	            if structure.get("schema") is None:
238	                structure["schema"] = {}
239	
240	    def _from_context(
241	        self, ctx: CompiledGrammar, key_string: str, grammar_stats: GrammarStats
242	    ) -> XGrammarGrammar:
243	        matcher = GrammarMatcher(
244	            ctx,
245	            max_rollback_tokens=MAX_ROLLBACK_TOKENS,
246	            override_stop_tokens=self.override_stop_tokens,
247	        )
248	        return XGrammarGrammar(
249	            matcher,
250	            self.vocab_size,
251	            ctx,
252	            self.override_stop_tokens,
253	            key_string,
254	            grammar_stats,
255	        )
256	
257	    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
258	        try:
259	            if key_string == "$$ANY$$":
260	                # Note: This builtin JSON grammar includes *all* valid JSON (including, for example, arrays at the root)
261	                ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_builtin_json_grammar()
262	            else:
263	                ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_json_schema(
264	                    schema=key_string, any_whitespace=self.any_whitespace
265	                )
266	
267	        except (RuntimeError, json.decoder.JSONDecodeError, UnicodeDecodeError) as e:
268	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid json_schema: {key_string=}, {e=}")
269	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
270	        return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="json"))
271	
272	    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
273	        try:
274	            ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_grammar(key_string)
275	        except RuntimeError as e:
276	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid ebnf: {key_string=}, {e=}")
277	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
278	        return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="ebnf"))
279	
280	    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
281	        try:
282	            ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_regex(key_string)
283	        except RuntimeError as e:
284	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid regex: {key_string=}, {e=}")
285	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
286	        return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="regex"))
287	
288	    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
289	        try:
290	            # TODO(dark): it's REALLY stupid to construct object from string and decode it again
291	            structural_tag = json.loads(key_string)
292	            if is_legacy_structural_tag(structural_tag):
293	                self._sanitize_structural_tag_structures(structural_tag)
294	                tags = [
295	                    StructuralTagItem(
296	                        begin=structure["begin"],
297	                        schema=json.dumps(structure["schema"]),
298	                        end=structure["end"],
299	                    )
300	                    for structure in structural_tag["structures"]
301	                ]
302	                ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_structural_tag(
303	                    tags, structural_tag["triggers"]
304	                )
305	            else:
306	                format_dict = structural_tag.get("format")
307	                if isinstance(format_dict, dict):
308	                    self._sanitize_structural_format(format_dict)
309	                    structural_tag["format"] = format_dict
310	                    key_string = json.dumps(structural_tag)
311	                ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_structural_tag(key_string)
312	        except (RuntimeError, json.decoder.JSONDecodeError) as e:
313	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid structural_tag: {key_string=}, {e=}")
314	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
315	        return self._from_context(
316	            ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="structural_tag")
317	        )
318	
319	    def reset(self):
320	        self.grammar_compiler.clear_cache()
321	
322	
323	def demo_test():
324	    from transformers import AutoConfig, AutoTokenizer
325	
326	    from sglang.test.test_utils import DEFAULT_MODEL_NAME_FOR_TEST
327	
328	    tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(DEFAULT_MODEL_NAME_FOR_TEST)
329	    hf_config = AutoConfig.from_pretrained(DEFAULT_MODEL_NAME_FOR_TEST)
330	
331	    # Should use vocab size from model config
332	    vocab_size = hf_config.vocab_size
333	    eos_token_id = tokenizer.eos_token_id
334	
335	    backend = XGrammarGrammarBackend(
336	        tokenizer, vocab_size=vocab_size, model_eos_token_ids=[eos_token_id]
337	    )
338	    regex = r"hello (world|there)"
339	    grammar = backend.dispatch_regex(regex)
340	    tokens = [
341	        tokenizer.encode(t, add_special_tokens=False)[0] for t in ["hello", " world"]
342	    ]
343	
344	    # Test termination
345	    grammar.accept_token(tokens[0])  # accept "hello"
346	    grammar.accept_token(tokens[1])  # accept " world"
347	    grammar.accept_token(eos_token_id)  # accept EOS
348	    assert grammar.is_terminated()
349	
350	    # Test rollback the terminated state
351	    grammar.rollback(1)
352	    assert not grammar.is_terminated()
353	
354	
355	if __name__ == "__main__":
356	    demo_test()
357

42	def run_internal(state, program, func_args, func_kwargs, sync):
43	    try:
44	        state.ret_value = program.func(state, *func_args, **func_kwargs)
45	    except Exception as e:
46	        raise e
47	    finally:
48	        state.stream_executor.end()
49	
50	    if sync:
51	        state.stream_executor.sync()
52	
53	    if global_config.verbosity >= 2:
54	        print(state.text())
55	
56	
57	def run_program(
58	    program,
59	    backend,
60	    func_args,
61	    func_kwargs,
62	    default_sampling_para,
63	    stream,
64	    sync=False,
65	    use_thread=True,
66	):
67	    if hasattr(backend, "endpoint"):
68	        backend = backend.endpoint
69	    assert backend is not None, "Please specify a backend"
70	    func_kwargs.update(program.bind_arguments)
71	    stream_executor = StreamExecutor(
72	        backend,
73	        func_kwargs,
74	        default_sampling_para,
75	        chat_template=None,
76	        stream=stream,
77	        num_api_spec_tokens=program.num_api_spec_tokens,
78	        use_thread=use_thread,
79	    )
80	    state = ProgramState(stream_executor)
81	
82	    if stream:
83	        t = threading.Thread(
84	            target=run_internal, args=(state, program, func_args, func_kwargs, sync)
85	        )
86	        t.start()
87	        return state
88	    else:
89	        run_internal(state, program, func_args, func_kwargs, sync)
90	        return state
91

93	def run_program_batch(

100	    generator_style=False,
101	):
102	    if hasattr(backend, "endpoint"):
103	        backend = backend.endpoint
104	
105	    # Pre-cache the common prefix for a batch. The prefix is extracted by tracing the program.
106	    if global_config.enable_precache_with_tracing and len(batch_arguments) > 1:
107	        cache_program(program, backend)
108	
109	    # Run all programs
110	    if num_threads == "auto":
111	        num_threads = max(96, multiprocessing.cpu_count() * 16)
112	    num_threads = min(num_threads, len(batch_arguments))
113	
114	    if generator_style:
115	        return _run_program_batch_generator(
116	            program,
117	            backend,
118	            batch_arguments,

123

16	import json
17	import logging
18	import os
19	from typing import List, Optional, Tuple
20	
21	import torch
22	from llguidance import LLMatcher, LLTokenizer, StructTag, grammar_from
23	from llguidance.hf import from_tokenizer
24	from llguidance.torch import (
25	    allocate_token_bitmask,
26	    apply_token_bitmask_inplace,
27	    fill_next_token_bitmask,
28	)
29	
30	from sglang.srt.constrained.base_grammar_backend import (
31	    INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ,
32	    BaseGrammarBackend,
33	    BaseGrammarObject,
34	)

39	
40	class GuidanceGrammar(BaseGrammarObject):
41	
42	    def __init__(self, llguidance_tokenizer: LLTokenizer, serialized_grammar: str):
43	        super().__init__()
44	        self.llguidance_tokenizer = llguidance_tokenizer
45	        self.serialized_grammar = serialized_grammar
46	
47	        self.ll_matcher = LLMatcher(
48	            self.llguidance_tokenizer,
49	            self.serialized_grammar,
50	            log_level=int(os.environ.get("LLGUIDANCE_LOG_LEVEL", "1")),
51	        )
52	        self.bitmask = None
53	
54	    def accept_token(self, token: int):
55	        if not self.ll_matcher.consume_token(token):
56	            logger.warning(f"matcher error: {self.ll_matcher.get_error()}")
57	            self.finished = True
58	
59	    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
60	        if self.ll_matcher.is_stopped():
61	            self.finished = True
62	
63	        fill_next_token_bitmask(self.ll_matcher, vocab_mask, idx)
64	
65	    def allocate_vocab_mask(
66	        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
67	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
68	        if self.bitmask is None or self.bitmask.shape[0] < batch_size:
69	            # only create bitmask when batch gets larger
70	            self.bitmask = allocate_token_bitmask(
71	                batch_size, self.llguidance_tokenizer.vocab_size
72	            )
73	            bitmask = self.bitmask
74	        else:
75	            bitmask = self.bitmask[:batch_size]
76	
77	        return bitmask
78	
79	    @staticmethod
80	    def move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor:
81	        return vocab_mask.to(device, non_blocking=True)
82	
83	    @staticmethod
84	    def apply_vocab_mask(logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor) -> None:
85	        apply_token_bitmask_inplace(logits, vocab_mask)
86	
87	    def copy(self):
88	        return GuidanceGrammar(
89	            llguidance_tokenizer=self.llguidance_tokenizer,
90	            serialized_grammar=self.serialized_grammar,
91	        )
92	
93	    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer) -> Optional[Tuple[List[int], str]]:
94	        ff_tokens = self.ll_matcher.compute_ff_tokens()
95	        if ff_tokens:
96	            return ff_tokens, ""
97	        else:
98	            return None
99	
100	    def jump_forward_str_state(self, helper: Tuple[List[int], str]) -> Tuple[str, int]:
101	        return "", -1
102	
103	    def jump_and_retokenize(
104	        self, old_output_ids: List[int], new_output_ids: List[int], next_state: int
105	    ):
106	        pass
107	
108	
109	class GuidanceBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
110	
111	    def __init__(
112	        self,
113	        tokenizer,
114	        any_whitespace: bool = True,
115	        whitespace_pattern: Optional[str] = None,
116	        n_vocab: Optional[int] = None,
117	    ):
118	        super().__init__()
119	
120	        self.tokenizer = tokenizer
121	        self.any_whitespace = any_whitespace
122	        self.whitespace_pattern = whitespace_pattern
123	        self.llguidance_tokenizer = from_tokenizer(self.tokenizer, n_vocab)
124	
125	    def _from_serialized(self, serialized_grammar) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
126	        try:
127	            return GuidanceGrammar(
128	                llguidance_tokenizer=self.llguidance_tokenizer,
129	                serialized_grammar=serialized_grammar,
130	            )
131	        except Exception as e:
132	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid grammar: {serialized_grammar=}, {e=}")
133	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
134	
135	    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
136	        try:
137	            serialized_grammar = LLMatcher.grammar_from_json_schema(
138	                key_string,
139	                defaults={
140	                    "whitespace_flexible": self.any_whitespace,
141	                    "whitespace_pattern": self.whitespace_pattern,
142	                },
143	            )
144	        except Exception as e:
145	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid json_schema: {key_string=}, {e=}")
146	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
147	        return self._from_serialized(serialized_grammar)
148	
149	    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
150	        serialized_grammar = grammar_from("regex", key_string)
151	        return self._from_serialized(serialized_grammar)
152	
153	    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
154	        try:
155	            serialized_grammar = grammar_from("ebnf", key_string)
156	            return self._from_serialized(serialized_grammar)
157	        except ValueError as e:
158	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid ebnf: {key_string=}, {e=}")
159	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
160	
161	    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
162	        try:
163	            structural_tag = json.loads(key_string)
164	            assert is_legacy_structural_tag(structural_tag)
165	            tags = [
166	                StructTag(
167	                    begin=structure["begin"],
168	                    grammar=structure["schema"],
169	                    end=structure["end"],
170	                    trigger=structural_tag["triggers"][0],  # TODO?
171	                )
172	                for structure in structural_tag["structures"]
173	            ]
174	            g = StructTag.to_grammar(tags)
175	            return self._from_serialized(g)
176	        except Exception as e:
177	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid structural_tag: {key_string=}, {e=}")
178	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ

27	class ReasonerGrammarObject(BaseGrammarObject):
28	    def __init__(self, grammar: BaseGrammarObject, think_end_id: int):
29	        super().__init__()
30	        self.grammar = grammar
31	        self.think_end_id = think_end_id
32	        # -1    means thinking has not ended yet
33	        # 0     means just ended thinking in the last token
34	        # +     means number of tokens after thinking ended
35	        self.tokens_after_think_end = -1
36	
37	    def maybe_init_reasoning(self, reasoning: bool):
38	        self.tokens_after_think_end = -1 if reasoning else 0
39	
40	    def transfer_state(self, token: int) -> int:
41	        if self.tokens_after_think_end == -1 and token == self.think_end_id:
42	            self.tokens_after_think_end = 0
43	        elif self.tokens_after_think_end >= 0:
44	            self.tokens_after_think_end += 1
45	
46	    def rollback_state(self):
47	        if self.tokens_after_think_end == 0:
48	            self.tokens_after_think_end = -1
49	        elif self.tokens_after_think_end > 0:
50	            self.tokens_after_think_end -= 1
51	
52	    def accept_token(self, token: int):
53	        if self.tokens_after_think_end >= 0:
54	            self.grammar.accept_token(token)
55	        self.transfer_state(token)
56	
57	    def is_terminated(self):
58	        return self.grammar.is_terminated()
59	
60	    def rollback(self, k):
61	        steps_after_think = min(k, self.tokens_after_think_end)
62	        if steps_after_think > 0:
63	            self.grammar.rollback(steps_after_think)
64	
65	        for _ in range(k):
66	            self.rollback_state()
67	
68	    def allocate_vocab_mask(
69	        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
70	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
71	        return self.grammar.allocate_vocab_mask(vocab_size, batch_size, device)
72	
73	    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
74	        if self.tokens_after_think_end >= 0:
75	            self.grammar.fill_vocab_mask(vocab_mask, idx)
76	
77	    def move_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor:
78	        return self.grammar.move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask, device)
79	
80	    @property
81	    def apply_vocab_mask(self):
82	        return self.grammar.apply_vocab_mask
83	
84	    def copy(self) -> BaseGrammarObject:
85	        return ReasonerGrammarObject(self.grammar.copy(), self.think_end_id)
86	
87	    @property
88	    def finished(self):
89	        return self.grammar.finished
90	
91	    @finished.setter
92	    def finished(self, finished):
93	        self.grammar.finished = finished
94	
95	    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer):
96	        return self.grammar.try_jump_forward(tokenizer)
97	
98	    def jump_forward_str_state(self, helper):
99	        return self.grammar.jump_forward_str_state(helper)
100	
101	    def jump_and_retokenize(
102	        self, old_output_ids: List[int], new_output_ids: List[int], next_state: int
103	    ):
104	        return self.grammar.jump_and_retokenize(
105	            old_output_ids, new_output_ids, next_state
106	        )

109	class ReasonerGrammarBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
110	    def __init__(self, grammar_backend: BaseGrammarBackend, think_end_id):
111	        super().__init__()
112	        self.grammar_backend = grammar_backend
113	        self.think_end_id = think_end_id
114	
115	    def _init_value_dispatch(
116	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], reasoning: bool
117	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
118	        ret = self.grammar_backend._init_value_dispatch(key, reasoning)
119	        # avoid wrapping invalid grammar, so that the scheduler can detect it
120	        if ret is None or ret is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:
121	            return ret
122	        obj = ReasonerGrammarObject(ret, self.think_end_id)
123	        obj.maybe_init_reasoning(reasoning)
124	        return obj

40	# Env var was set in sglang.srt.server_args.ServerArgs.__post_init__
41	DISABLE_DISK_CACHE = get_bool_env_var("SGLANG_DISABLE_OUTLINES_DISK_CACHE", "true")
42	
43	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
44	
45	
46	@dataclasses.dataclass
47	class JumpEdge:
48	    symbol: str = None
49	    symbol_next_state: int = None
50	    byte: int = None
51	    byte_next_state: int = None
52	
53	
54	def disk_cache(expire: Optional[float] = None, typed=False, ignore=()):
55	    if not DISABLE_DISK_CACHE:
56	        return cache(expire, typed, ignore)
57	    else:
58	        return lambda fn: None
59	
60	
61	@disk_cache()
62	def init_state_to_jump_forward(regex_string):
63	    try:
64	        regex_pattern = interegular.parse_pattern(regex_string)
65	    except InvalidSyntax as e:
66	        logger.warning(f"skip invalid regex: {regex_string}, {e=}")
67	        return
68	
69	    byte_fsm = make_byte_level_fsm(regex_pattern.to_fsm().reduce(), keep_utf8=True)
70	    regex_fsm, _ = make_deterministic_fsm(byte_fsm)
71	
72	    fsm_info: FSMInfo = regex_fsm.fsm_info
73	
74	    symbol_to_id = fsm_info.alphabet_symbol_mapping
75	    id_to_symbol = {}
76	    for symbol, id_ in symbol_to_id.items():
77	        id_to_symbol.setdefault(id_, []).append(symbol)
78	
79	    transitions = fsm_info.transitions
80	
81	    outgoings_ct = defaultdict(int)
82	    # NOTE(lsyin): Final states can lead to terminate, so they have one outgoing edge naturally
83	    for s in fsm_info.finals:
84	        outgoings_ct[s] = 1
85	
86	    state_to_jump_forward = {}
87	    for (state, id_), next_state in transitions.items():
88	        if id_ == fsm_info.alphabet_anything_value:
89	            # Arbitrarily symbol cannot be recognized as jump forward
90	            continue
91	
92	        symbols = id_to_symbol[id_]
93	        for c in symbols:
94	            if len(c) > 1:
95	                # Skip byte level transitions like c = "5E"
96	                continue
97	
98	            outgoings_ct[state] += 1
99	            if outgoings_ct[state] > 1:
100	                if state in state_to_jump_forward:
101	                    del state_to_jump_forward[state]
102	                break
103	
104	            state_to_jump_forward[state] = JumpEdge(
105	                symbol=c,
106	                symbol_next_state=next_state,
107	            )
108	
109	    # Process the byte level jump forward
110	    outgoings_ct = defaultdict(int)
111	    for s in fsm_info.finals:
112	        outgoings_ct[s] = 1
113	
114	    for (state, id_), next_state in transitions.items():
115	        if id_ == fsm_info.alphabet_anything_value:
116	            continue
117	        symbols = id_to_symbol[id_]
118	        for c in symbols:
119	            byte_ = None
120	            if len(c) == 1 and ord(c) < 0x80:
121	                # ASCII character
122	                byte_ = ord(c)
123	            elif len(c) > 1:
124	                # FIXME: This logic is due to the leading \x00
125	                # https://github.com/outlines-dev/outlines/pull/930
126	                byte_ = int(symbols[0][1:], 16)
127	
128	            if byte_ is not None:
129	                outgoings_ct[state] += 1
130	                if outgoings_ct[state] > 1:
131	                    if state in state_to_jump_forward:
132	                        del state_to_jump_forward[state]
133	                    break
134	                e = state_to_jump_forward.get(state, JumpEdge())
135	                e.byte = byte_
136	                e.byte_next_state = next_state
137	                state_to_jump_forward[state] = e
138	
139	    return state_to_jump_forward
140	
141	
142	class OutlinesJumpForwardMap:
143	    def __init__(self, regex_string):
144	        self.state_to_jump_forward = init_state_to_jump_forward(regex_string)
145	
146	    def jump_forward_symbol(self, state):
147	        jump_forward_str = ""
148	        next_state = state
149	        while state in self.state_to_jump_forward:
150	            e = self.state_to_jump_forward[state]
151	            if e.symbol is None:
152	                break
153	            jump_forward_str += e.symbol
154	            next_state = e.symbol_next_state
155	            state = next_state
156	
157	        return jump_forward_str, next_state
158	
159	    def jump_forward_byte(self, state):
160	        if state not in self.state_to_jump_forward:
161	            return None
162	
163	        jump_forward_bytes = []
164	        next_state = None
165	        while state in self.state_to_jump_forward:
166	            e = self.state_to_jump_forward[state]
167	            assert e.byte is not None and e.byte_next_state is not None
168	            jump_forward_bytes.append((e.byte, e.byte_next_state))
169	            next_state = e.byte_next_state
170	            state = next_state
171	
172	        return jump_forward_bytes
173	
174	    def is_jump_forward_symbol_state(self, state):
175	        return (
176	            state in self.state_to_jump_forward
177	            and self.state_to_jump_forward[state].symbol is not None
178	        )
179

181	def test_main(regex_string):
182	    jump_forward_map = OutlinesJumpForwardMap(regex_string)
183	    for state, e in jump_forward_map.state_to_jump_forward.items():
184	        if e.symbol is not None:
185	            jump_forward_str, next_state = jump_forward_map.jump_forward_symbol(state)
186	            print(f"{state} -> {next_state}", jump_forward_str)
187	        bytes_ = jump_forward_map.jump_forward_byte(state)
188	        print(f"{state} -> {bytes_[-1][1]}", [hex(b) for b, _ in bytes_])
189	
190

192	    import outlines

194	    outlines.caching.clear_cache()
195	    test_main(r"The google's DNS sever address is " + IP_REGEX)
196	    test_main(r"霍格沃茨特快列车|霍比特人比尔博")
197	    # 霍格: \xe9\x9c\x8d \xe6\xa0\xbc ...
198	    # 霍比: \xe9\x9c\x8d \xe6\xaf\x94 ...

## SGLang Constraint System - Complete End-to-End Implementation Guide

I've thoroughly analyzed the SGLang codebase to understand how constraints (json_schema, regex, ebnf) are currently implemented. Here's a comprehensive report focusing on how you can implement a `json_path` constraint:

---

### **1. HIGH-LEVEL ARCHITECTURE**

The constraint system works through multiple layers:

```
User API (lang/api.py) 
    ↓
IR Definition (lang/ir.py: SglGen, SglSamplingParams)
    ↓
Sampling Params (srt/sampling/sampling_params.py: SamplingParams)
    ↓
Grammar Manager (srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py: GrammarManager)
    ↓
Grammar Backend Factory (srt/constrained/base_grammar_backend.py)
    ↓
Specific Backends (outlines, xgrammar, llguidance)
```

---

### **2. KEY FILES AND LOCATIONS**

**Path:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493191991-5su1ov/python/sglang/`

| Component | File | Key Classes |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| **User API** | `lang/api.py` | `gen()` function |
| **IR Layer** | `lang/ir.py` | `SglSamplingParams`, `SglGen` |
| **Runtime Sampling** | `srt/sampling/sampling_params.py` | `SamplingParams` |
| **Grammar Management** | `srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py` | `GrammarManager` |
| **Base Backend** | `srt/constrained/base_grammar_backend.py` | `BaseGrammarBackend`, `BaseGrammarObject` |
| **Outlines Backend** | `srt/constrained/outlines_backend.py` | `OutlinesGrammarBackend`, `OutlinesGrammar` |
| **XGrammar Backend** | `srt/constrained/xgrammar_backend.py` | `XGrammarGrammarBackend`, `XGrammarGrammar` |
| **LLGuidance Backend** | `srt/constrained/llguidance_backend.py` | `GuidanceBackend`, `GuidanceGrammar` |
| **OpenAI Protocol** | `srt/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py` | `CompletionRequest` |
| **Interpreter** | `lang/interpreter.py` | Sampling params merge list |

---

### **3. EXISTING CONSTRAINT IMPLEMENTATION - STEP BY STEP**

#### **Step 1: User API Level (lang/api.py)**

The `gen()` function accepts constraint parameters:

```python
def gen(
    name: Optional[str] = None,
    # ... other params ...
    regex: Optional[str] = None,
    json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
):
    # For json_schema/regex, creates SglGen with constraint

        name,
        # ... params ...

        json_schema=json_schema,
```


---

#### **Step 2: IR Layer (lang/ir.py)**

The `SglSamplingParams` dataclass stores all sampling configuration:

```python
@dataclasses.dataclass
class SglSamplingParams:
    max_new_tokens: int = 128
    # ... other params ...
    json_schema: Optional[str] = None
    
    # for constrained generation, not included in to_xxx_kwargs
    dtype: Optional[str] = None

```

The `SglGen` class receives constraint via `SglSamplingParams`:

```python

```


And add to `SglGen.__init__`:
```python

```

---

#### **Step 3: Sampling Parameters Layer (srt/sampling/sampling_params.py)**

The runtime `SamplingParams` class stores constraints and validates them:

```python
class SamplingParams:
    def __init__(
        self,
        # ... other params ...
        json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
        regex: Optional[str] = None,
        ebnf: Optional[str] = None,
        structural_tag: Optional[str] = None,
        # ... more params ...
    ):
        self.json_schema = json_schema
        self.regex = regex
        self.ebnf = ebnf
        self.structural_tag = structural_tag
        
    def verify(self, vocab_size):
        # CRITICAL: Constraints are mutually exclusive!
        grammars = [
            self.json_schema,
            self.regex,
            self.ebnf,
        ]
        if sum(x is not None for x in grammars) > 1:
            raise ValueError("Only one of regex, json_schema, or ebnf can be set.")
```

1. Add field: `json_path: Optional[str] = None`
2. Update constraint validation to include `json_path` in the mutual exclusion check

---

#### **Step 4: Grammar Manager (srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py)**

The `GrammarManager` routes constraint requests to the appropriate backend:

```python
def process_req_with_grammar(self, req: Req) -> bool:
    if (
        req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None
        or req.sampling_params.regex is not None
        or req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None
        or req.sampling_params.structural_tag is not None
    ):
        if self.grammar_backend is None:
            error_msg = "Grammar-based generation not supported..."
            req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
        else:
            if req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None:
                key = ("json", req.sampling_params.json_schema)
            elif req.sampling_params.regex is not None:
                key = ("regex", req.sampling_params.regex)
            elif req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None:
                key = ("ebnf", req.sampling_params.ebnf)
            elif req.sampling_params.structural_tag:
                key = ("structural_tag", req.sampling_params.structural_tag)
```

```python
    key = ("json_path", req.sampling_params.json_path)
```

---

#### **Step 5: Base Grammar Backend (srt/constrained/base_grammar_backend.py)**

The `BaseGrammarBackend` defines the interface that all backends must implement:

```python
class BaseGrammarBackend:
    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
        return self._not_supported("json", key_string)
    
    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
        return self._not_supported("regex", key_string)
    
    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
        return self._not_supported("ebnf", key_string)
    
    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
        return self._not_supported("structural_tag", key_string)

    def _init_value_dispatch(
        self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
        s = time.perf_counter()
        key_type, key_string = key
        if key_type == "json":
            grammar = self.dispatch_json(key_string)
        elif key_type == "regex":
            grammar = self.dispatch_regex(key_string)
        elif key_type == "ebnf":
            grammar = self.dispatch_ebnf(key_string)
        elif key_type == "structural_tag":
            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)
        # ... and more dispatch types ...
```

**For json_path:** Add method:
```python
def dispatch_json_path(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
    return self._not_supported("json_path", key_string)
```

And add to `_init_value_dispatch`:
```python

    grammar = self.dispatch_json_path(key_string)
```

#### **Step 6: Specific Backend Implementations**

Each backend (Outlines, XGrammar, LLGuidance) implements the dispatch methods.

##### **6a. Outlines Backend (outlines_backend.py)**

```python
class OutlinesGrammarBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
    def __init__(self, tokenizer, whitespace_pattern: str | None):
        super().__init__()
        self.outlines_tokenizer = TransformerTokenizer(tokenizer)
        self.whitespace_pattern = whitespace_pattern
    
    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str):
        try:
            regex = build_regex_from_object(
                key_string,
                whitespace_pattern=self.whitespace_pattern,
            )
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"Hit invalid json_schema: {key_string=}, {e=}")
            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
        return self._compile_regex(regex)
    
    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str):
        return self._compile_regex(key_string)
    
    def _compile_regex(self, regex: str) -> Optional[OutlinesGrammar]:
        try:
            guide = RegexGuide.from_regex(regex, self.outlines_tokenizer)
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"Hit invalid regex schema: {regex=}, {e=}")
            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
        return OutlinesGrammar(guide, jump_forward_map=None)
```

**For json_path in Outlines:** Add:
```python
def dispatch_json_path(self, key_string: str):
    try:
        # Convert json_path to regex constraint
        # Example: json_path="$.users[*].name" -> regex for matching that path
        regex = convert_json_path_to_regex(key_string)
    except Exception as e:
        logger.error(f"Hit invalid json_path: {key_string=}, {e=}")
        return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
    return self._compile_regex(regex)
```

##### **6b. XGrammar Backend (xgrammar_backend.py)**

```python
class XGrammarGrammarBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
        try:
            if key_string == "$$ANY$$":
                ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_builtin_json_grammar()
            else:
                ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_json_schema(
                    schema=key_string, any_whitespace=self.any_whitespace
                )
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"Hit invalid json_schema: {key_string=}, {e=}")
            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
        return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="json"))

    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
        try:
            ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_regex(key_string)
        except RuntimeError as e:
            logger.error(f"Hit invalid regex: {key_string=}, {e=}")
            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
        return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="regex"))
```

**For json_path in XGrammar:** Add:
```python
def dispatch_json_path(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
    try:
        # Convert json_path to EBNF or regex
        ebnf_or_regex = convert_json_path_to_ebnf(key_string)
        ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_grammar(ebnf_or_regex)
    except Exception as e:
        logger.error(f"Hit invalid json_path: {key_string=}, {e=}")
        return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
    return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="json_path"))
```

##### **6c. LLGuidance Backend (llguidance_backend.py)**

```python
class GuidanceBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):

        try:

                key_string,
                defaults={
                    "whitespace_flexible": self.any_whitespace,
                    "whitespace_pattern": self.whitespace_pattern,
                },
            )
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"Hit invalid json_schema: {key_string=}, {e=}")
            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
        return self._from_serialized(serialized_grammar)
```

### **4. CONSTRAINT EXECUTION FLOW - DETAILED DIAGRAM**

```
Request with json_path="$.users[*].name"
    ↓
CompletionRequest.json_path validated
    ↓
GrammarManager.process_req_with_grammar()
    ↓ Creates key ("json_path", "$.users[*].name")
    ↓
BaseGrammarBackend.get_cached_or_future_value()
    ↓ Submits to ThreadPoolExecutor
    ↓
BaseGrammarBackend._init_value_dispatch()
    ↓
Backend-specific dispatch_json_path()
    ↓
Convert json_path → regex/EBNF/serialized grammar
    ↓
Backend-specific Grammar Object (OutlinesGrammar/XGrammarGrammar/GuidanceGrammar)
    ↓
Stored in cache with key ("json_path", "...")
    ↓
Request waits for grammar to be ready
    ↓
During token generation:
    grammar.allocate_vocab_mask() - create token mask
    grammar.fill_vocab_mask() - determine allowed tokens
    grammar.apply_vocab_mask() - mask logits
    grammar.accept_token() - track grammar state
```

---

### **5. GRAMMAR OBJECT INTERFACE (BaseGrammarObject)**

All grammar implementations must implement this interface:

```python
class BaseGrammarObject:
    def accept_token(self, token: int) -> None:
        """Accept a token and update state."""
        raise NotImplementedError()
    
    def allocate_vocab_mask(
        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
    ) -> torch.Tensor:
        """Allocate vocabulary mask tensor."""
        raise NotImplementedError()
    
    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
        """Fill mask with allowed token IDs for current state."""
        raise NotImplementedError()
    
    @staticmethod
    def apply_vocab_mask(logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor) -> None:
        """Apply mask to logits (set disallowed to -inf)."""
        raise NotImplementedError()
    
    def copy(self) -> "BaseGrammarObject":
        """Create independent copy for batch processing."""
        return self
    
    def rollback(self, k: int):
        """Rollback last k tokens."""
        raise NotImplementedError()
    
    def is_terminated(self):
        """Check if grammar accepts only EOS."""
        return False
    
    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer) -> Optional[Tuple[List[int], str]]:
        """Optional: Return tokens to jump forward if possible."""
        raise NotImplementedError()
```

2. **lang/ir.py**:

4. **srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py**:

5. **srt/constrained/base_grammar_backend.py**:

6. **srt/constrained/outlines_backend.py**:

7. **srt/constrained/xgrammar_backend.py**:

8. **srt/constrained/llguidance_backend.py**:

9. **srt/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py**:

10. **lang/interpreter.py**:
    - Add `"json_path"` to the sampling params merge list (around line 809)

---

### **7. KEY INSIGHTS FOR YOUR IMPLEMENTATION**

1. **Constraints are Mutually Exclusive**: Only one of `json_schema`, `regex`, `ebnf`, `structural_tag` (and your new `json_path`) can be set at once.

2. **Backend Dispatch Pattern**: The routing is done by key type in a tuple `(type_name, constraint_string)`. Each backend implements dispatch methods for each type.

3. **Grammar Objects Must Be Stateful**: They track position in the FSM and can accept/rollback tokens.

4. **Caching**: Constraints are cached after compilation to avoid recompilation. The cache key is `(type_name, constraint_string)`.

5. **Error Handling**: Invalid constraints return `INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ`, which triggers request abortion with a user-friendly error message.

6. **Conversion Strategy**: Since most backends can work with regex or EBNF:
   - **For Outlines**: Convert `json_path` to regex pattern
   - **For XGrammar**: Convert `json_path` to EBNF grammar
   - **For LLGuidance**: Convert `json_path` to serialized grammar format

7. **Jump Forward Optimization**: The `try_jump_forward()` method is optional but important for performance - it allows skipping multiple tokens when the grammar has a deterministic path.

## SGLang Constraint System - Complete End-to-End Implementation Guide

I've thoroughly analyzed the SGLang codebase to understand how constraints (json_schema, regex, ebnf) are currently implemented. Here's a comprehensive report focusing on how you can implement a `json_path` constraint:

---

### **1. HIGH-LEVEL ARCHITECTURE**

The constraint system works through multiple layers:

```
User API (lang/api.py) 
    ↓
IR Definition (lang/ir.py: SglGen, SglSamplingParams)
    ↓
Sampling Params (srt/sampling/sampling_params.py: SamplingParams)

Grammar Manager (srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py: GrammarManager)
    ↓
Grammar Backend Factory (srt/constrained/base_grammar_backend.py)
    ↓
Specific Backends (outlines, xgrammar, llguidance)
```

---

### **2. KEY FILES AND LOCATIONS**

**Path:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493191991-5su1ov/python/sglang/`

| Component | File | Key Classes |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| **User API** | `lang/api.py` | `gen()` function |
| **IR Layer** | `lang/ir.py` | `SglSamplingParams`, `SglGen` |
| **Runtime Sampling** | `srt/sampling/sampling_params.py` | `SamplingParams` |
| **Grammar Management** | `srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py` | `GrammarManager` |
| **Base Backend** | `srt/constrained/base_grammar_backend.py` | `BaseGrammarBackend`, `BaseGrammarObject` |
| **Outlines Backend** | `srt/constrained/outlines_backend.py` | `OutlinesGrammarBackend`, `OutlinesGrammar` |
| **XGrammar Backend** | `srt/constrained/xgrammar_backend.py` | `XGrammarGrammarBackend`, `XGrammarGrammar` |
| **LLGuidance Backend** | `srt/constrained/llguidance_backend.py` | `GuidanceBackend`, `GuidanceGrammar` |
| **OpenAI Protocol** | `srt/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py` | `CompletionRequest` |
| **Interpreter** | `lang/interpreter.py` | Sampling params merge list |

### **3. EXISTING CONSTRAINT IMPLEMENTATION - STEP BY STEP**

#### **Step 1: User API Level (lang/api.py)**

The `gen()` function accepts constraint parameters:

```python
    name: Optional[str] = None,

):

        name,
        # ... params ...
```

#### **Step 2: IR Layer (lang/ir.py)**

The `SglSamplingParams` dataclass stores all sampling configuration:

```python
@dataclasses.dataclass
class SglSamplingParams:
    max_new_tokens: int = 128
    # ... other params ...
    json_schema: Optional[str] = None
    
    # for constrained generation, not included in to_xxx_kwargs

```

The `SglGen` class receives constraint via `SglSamplingParams`:

```python
class SglGen(SglExpr):
    def __init__(

    ):

            # ... other params ...

        )
```

And add to `SglGen.__init__`:


---

#### **Step 3: Sampling Parameters Layer (srt/sampling/sampling_params.py)**

The runtime `SamplingParams` class stores constraints and validates them:

```python
class SamplingParams:
    def __init__(
        self,
        # ... other params ...
        json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
        regex: Optional[str] = None,
        ebnf: Optional[str] = None,
        structural_tag: Optional[str] = None,
        # ... more params ...
    ):
        self.json_schema = json_schema
        self.regex = regex
        self.ebnf = ebnf
        self.structural_tag = structural_tag
        
    def verify(self, vocab_size):
        # CRITICAL: Constraints are mutually exclusive!
        grammars = [
            self.json_schema,
            self.regex,
            self.ebnf,
        ]
        if sum(x is not None for x in grammars) > 1:
            raise ValueError("Only one of regex, json_schema, or ebnf can be set.")
```

#### **Step 4: Grammar Manager (srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py)**

The `GrammarManager` routes constraint requests to the appropriate backend:

```python
def process_req_with_grammar(self, req: Req) -> bool:
    if (
        req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None
        or req.sampling_params.regex is not None
        or req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None
        or req.sampling_params.structural_tag is not None
    ):
        if self.grammar_backend is None:
            error_msg = "Grammar-based generation not supported..."
            req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
        else:
            if req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None:
                key = ("json", req.sampling_params.json_schema)
            elif req.sampling_params.regex is not None:
                key = ("regex", req.sampling_params.regex)
            elif req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None:
                key = ("ebnf", req.sampling_params.ebnf)
            elif req.sampling_params.structural_tag:
                key = ("structural_tag", req.sampling_params.structural_tag)
```

#### **Step 5: Base Grammar Backend (srt/constrained/base_grammar_backend.py)**

The `BaseGrammarBackend` defines the interface that all backends must implement:

```python
class BaseGrammarBackend:
    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
        return self._not_supported("json", key_string)
    
    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
        return self._not_supported("regex", key_string)
    
    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
        return self._not_supported("ebnf", key_string)
    
    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
        return self._not_supported("structural_tag", key_string)

    def _init_value_dispatch(
        self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
        s = time.perf_counter()
        key_type, key_string = key
        if key_type == "json":
            grammar = self.dispatch_json(key_string)
        elif key_type == "regex":
            grammar = self.dispatch_regex(key_string)
        elif key_type == "ebnf":
            grammar = self.dispatch_ebnf(key_string)
        elif key_type == "structural_tag":
            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)
        # ... and more dispatch types ...
```


#### **Step 6: Specific Backend Implementations**

Each backend (Outlines, XGrammar, LLGuidance) implements the dispatch methods.

##### **6a. Outlines Backend (outlines_backend.py)**

```python
class OutlinesGrammarBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
    def __init__(self, tokenizer, whitespace_pattern: str | None):
        super().__init__()
        self.outlines_tokenizer = TransformerTokenizer(tokenizer)
        self.whitespace_pattern = whitespace_pattern
    
    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str):
        try:
            regex = build_regex_from_object(
                key_string,
                whitespace_pattern=self.whitespace_pattern,
            )
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"Hit invalid json_schema: {key_string=}, {e=}")
            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
        return self._compile_regex(regex)
    
    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str):
        return self._compile_regex(key_string)
    
    def _compile_regex(self, regex: str) -> Optional[OutlinesGrammar]:
        try:
            guide = RegexGuide.from_regex(regex, self.outlines_tokenizer)
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"Hit invalid regex schema: {regex=}, {e=}")
            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
        return OutlinesGrammar(guide, jump_forward_map=None)
```

**For json_path in Outlines:** Add:
```python
def dispatch_json_path(self, key_string: str):
    try:
        # Convert json_path to regex constraint
        # Example: json_path="$.users[*].name" -> regex for matching that path
        regex = convert_json_path_to_regex(key_string)
    except Exception as e:
        logger.error(f"Hit invalid json_path: {key_string=}, {e=}")
        return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
    return self._compile_regex(regex)
```

##### **6b. XGrammar Backend (xgrammar_backend.py)**

```python
class XGrammarGrammarBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
        try:
            if key_string == "$$ANY$$":
                ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_builtin_json_grammar()
            else:
                ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_json_schema(
                    schema=key_string, any_whitespace=self.any_whitespace
                )
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"Hit invalid json_schema: {key_string=}, {e=}")
            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
        return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="json"))

    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
        try:
            ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_regex(key_string)
        except RuntimeError as e:
            logger.error(f"Hit invalid regex: {key_string=}, {e=}")
            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
        return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="regex"))
```

**For json_path in XGrammar:** Add:
```python
def dispatch_json_path(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
    try:
        # Convert json_path to EBNF or regex
        ebnf_or_regex = convert_json_path_to_ebnf(key_string)
        ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_grammar(ebnf_or_regex)
    except Exception as e:
        logger.error(f"Hit invalid json_path: {key_string=}, {e=}")
        return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
    return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="json_path"))
```

##### **6c. LLGuidance Backend (llguidance_backend.py)**

```python
class GuidanceBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
        try:
            serialized_grammar = LLMatcher.grammar_from_json_schema(
                key_string,
                defaults={
                    "whitespace_flexible": self.any_whitespace,
                    "whitespace_pattern": self.whitespace_pattern,
                },
            )
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"Hit invalid json_schema: {key_string=}, {e=}")
            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
        return self._from_serialized(serialized_grammar)
```

**For json_path in LLGuidance:** Add:


### **4. CONSTRAINT EXECUTION FLOW - DETAILED DIAGRAM**

```
Request with json_path="$.users[*].name"
    ↓
CompletionRequest.json_path validated
    ↓
GrammarManager.process_req_with_grammar()
    ↓ Creates key ("json_path", "$.users[*].name")
    ↓
BaseGrammarBackend.get_cached_or_future_value()

BaseGrammarBackend._init_value_dispatch()

    ↓
Convert json_path → regex/EBNF/serialized grammar
    ↓
Backend-specific Grammar Object (OutlinesGrammar/XGrammarGrammar/GuidanceGrammar)
    ↓
Stored in cache with key ("json_path", "...")
    ↓
Request waits for grammar to be ready
    ↓
During token generation:
    grammar.allocate_vocab_mask() - create token mask
    grammar.fill_vocab_mask() - determine allowed tokens
    grammar.apply_vocab_mask() - mask logits
    grammar.accept_token() - track grammar state
```

### **5. GRAMMAR OBJECT INTERFACE (BaseGrammarObject)**

All grammar implementations must implement this interface:

```python
class BaseGrammarObject:
    def accept_token(self, token: int) -> None:
        """Accept a token and update state."""
        raise NotImplementedError()
    
    def allocate_vocab_mask(
        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
    ) -> torch.Tensor:
        """Allocate vocabulary mask tensor."""
        raise NotImplementedError()
    
    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
        """Fill mask with allowed token IDs for current state."""
        raise NotImplementedError()
    
    @staticmethod
    def apply_vocab_mask(logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor) -> None:
        """Apply mask to logits (set disallowed to -inf)."""
        raise NotImplementedError()
    
    def copy(self) -> "BaseGrammarObject":
        """Create independent copy for batch processing."""
        return self
    
    def rollback(self, k: int):
        """Rollback last k tokens."""
        raise NotImplementedError()
    
    def is_terminated(self):
        """Check if grammar accepts only EOS."""
        return False
    
    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer) -> Optional[Tuple[List[int], str]]:
        """Optional: Return tokens to jump forward if possible."""
        raise NotImplementedError()
```

109	
110	    # check regex is valid
111	    if regex is not None:
112	        try:
113	            re.compile(regex)
114	        except re.error as e:
115	            raise e
116	
117	    return SglGen(
118	        name,
119	        max_tokens,
120	        min_tokens,
121	        n,
122	        stop,
123	        stop_token_ids,
124	        stop_regex,
125	        temperature,

131	        ignore_eos,
132	        return_logprob,
133	        logprob_start_len,
134	        top_logprobs_num,
135	        return_text_in_logprobs,
136	        dtype,
137	        regex,
138	        json_schema,
139	    )

64	    def to_openai_kwargs(self):
65	        # OpenAI does not support top_k, so we drop it here
66	        if self.regex is not None:
67	            warnings.warn("Regular expression is not supported in the OpenAI backend.")
68	        return {
69	            "max_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
70	            "max_completion_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
71	            "n": self.n,
72	            "stop": self.stop or None,
73	            "temperature": self.temperature,
74	            "top_p": self.top_p,

76	            "presence_penalty": self.presence_penalty,

79	    def to_vertexai_kwargs(self):
80	        if self.regex is not None:

82	                "Regular expression is not supported in the VertexAI backend."
83	            )
84	        return {
85	            "candidate_count": 1,
86	            "max_output_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,

88	            "temperature": self.temperature,
89	            "top_p": self.top_p,

91	        }

141	class SglFunction:
142	    def __init__(self, func, num_api_spec_tokens=None, bind_arguments=None):
143	        self.func = func
144	        self.num_api_spec_tokens = num_api_spec_tokens
145	        self.bind_arguments = bind_arguments or {}
146	        self.pin_prefix_rid = None
147	
148	        # Parse arguments
149	        argspec = inspect.getfullargspec(func)
150	        assert argspec.args[0] == "s", 'The first argument must be "s"'
151	        self.arg_names = argspec.args[1:]
152	        self.arg_defaults = argspec.defaults if argspec.defaults is not None else []
153	
154	    def bind(self, **kwargs):
155	        assert all(key in self.arg_names for key in kwargs)
156	
157	        new_bind_dict = {**self.bind_arguments, **kwargs}
158	        return SglFunction(self.func, bind_arguments=new_bind_dict)
159	
160	    def run(
161	        self,
162	        *args,
163	        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
164	        n: int = 1,
165	        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
166	        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
167	        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
168	        temperature: float = 1.0,
169	        top_p: float = 1.0,
170	        top_k: int = -1,
171	        min_p: float = 0.0,
172	        frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
173	        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
174	        ignore_eos: bool = False,
175	        return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
176	        logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
177	        top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
178	        return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
179	        stream: bool = False,
180	        backend=None,
181	        use_thread: bool = True,
182	        **kwargs,
183	    ):
184	        from sglang.lang.interpreter import run_program
185	
186	        # avoid using [] as the default arg: https://nikos7am.com/posts/mutable-default-arguments/
187	        if stop is None:
188	            stop = []
189	        if stop_token_ids is None:
190	            stop_token_ids = []
191	        if stop_regex is None:
192	            stop_regex = []
193	
194	        default_sampling_para = SglSamplingParams(
195	            max_new_tokens=max_new_tokens,
196	            n=n,
197	            stop=stop,
198	            stop_token_ids=stop_token_ids,
199	            stop_regex=stop_regex,
200	            temperature=temperature,
201	            top_p=top_p,
202	            top_k=top_k,
203	            min_p=min_p,
204	            frequency_penalty=frequency_penalty,
205	            presence_penalty=presence_penalty,
206	            ignore_eos=ignore_eos,
207	            return_logprob=return_logprob,
208	            logprob_start_len=logprob_start_len,
209	            top_logprobs_num=top_logprobs_num,
210	            return_text_in_logprobs=return_text_in_logprobs,
211	        )
212	        backend = backend or global_config.default_backend
213	        return run_program(
214	            self,
215	            backend,
216	            args,
217	            kwargs,
218	            default_sampling_para,
219	            stream,
220	            use_thread=use_thread,
221	        )
222	
223	    def run_batch(
224	        self,
225	        batch_kwargs,
226	        *,
227	        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
228	        n: int = 1,
229	        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
230	        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
231	        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
232	        temperature: float = 1.0,
233	        top_p: float = 1.0,
234	        top_k: int = -1,
235	        min_p: float = 0.0,
236	        frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
237	        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
238	        ignore_eos: bool = False,
239	        return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
240	        logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
241	        top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
242	        return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
243	        backend=None,
244	        num_threads: Union[str, int] = "auto",
245	        progress_bar: bool = False,
246	        generator_style: bool = False,
247	    ):
248	        from sglang.lang.interpreter import run_program_batch
249	
250	        if stop is None:
251	            stop = []
252	        if stop_token_ids is None:
253	            stop_token_ids = []
254	        if stop_regex is None:
255	            stop_regex = []
256	
257	        assert isinstance(batch_kwargs, (list, tuple))
258	        if len(batch_kwargs) == 0:
259	            return []
260	        if not isinstance(batch_kwargs[0], dict):
261	            num_programs = len(batch_kwargs)
262	            # change the list of argument values to dict of arg_name -> arg_value
263	            batch_kwargs = [
264	                {self.arg_names[i]: v for i, v in enumerate(arg_values)}
265	                for arg_values in batch_kwargs
266	                if isinstance(arg_values, (list, tuple))
267	                and len(self.arg_names) - len(self.arg_defaults)
268	                <= len(arg_values)
269	                <= len(self.arg_names)
270	            ]
271	            # Ensure to raise an exception if the number of arguments mismatch
272	            if len(batch_kwargs) != num_programs:
273	                raise Exception("Given arguments mismatch the SGL function signature")
274	
275	        default_sampling_para = SglSamplingParams(
276	            max_new_tokens=max_new_tokens,
277	            n=n,
278	            stop=stop,
279	            stop_token_ids=stop_token_ids,
280	            stop_regex=stop_regex,
281	            temperature=temperature,
282	            top_p=top_p,
283	            top_k=top_k,
284	            min_p=min_p,
285	            frequency_penalty=frequency_penalty,
286	            presence_penalty=presence_penalty,
287	            ignore_eos=ignore_eos,
288	            return_logprob=return_logprob,
289	            logprob_start_len=logprob_start_len,
290	            top_logprobs_num=top_logprobs_num,
291	            return_text_in_logprobs=return_text_in_logprobs,
292	        )
293	        backend = backend or global_config.default_backend
294	        return run_program_batch(
295	            self,
296	            backend,
297	            batch_kwargs,
298	            default_sampling_para,
299	            num_threads,
300	            progress_bar,
301	            generator_style=generator_style,
302	        )

304	    def trace(self, *, backend=None, **kwargs):
305	        from sglang.lang.tracer import trace_program
306	
307	        backend = backend or global_config.default_backend
308	        return trace_program(self, kwargs, backend)
309	
310	    def cache(self, backend=None):
311	        from sglang.lang.interpreter import cache_program
312	
313	        backend = backend or global_config.default_backend
314	        return cache_program(self, backend)
315	
316	    def __call__(self, *args, **kwargs):
317	        from sglang.lang.tracer import TracingScope
318	
319	        tracing_scope = TracingScope.get_current_scope()
320	        if tracing_scope is None:
321	            return self.run(*args, **kwargs)
322	        else:
323	            kwargs["backend"] = tracing_scope.tracer_state.backend
324	            return self.trace(*args, **kwargs)

326	
327	class SglExpr:
328	    node_ct = 0
329	
330	    def __init__(self):
331	        self.node_id = SglExpr.node_ct
332	        self.prev_node = None
333	        self.pid = None
334	        SglExpr.node_ct += 1
335	
336	    def __add__(self, other):
337	        if isinstance(other, str):
338	            other = SglConstantText(other)
339	        assert isinstance(other, SglExpr)
340	
341	        return self.concatenate_ir(self, other)
342	
343	    def __radd__(self, other):
344	        if isinstance(other, str):
345	            other = SglConstantText(other)
346	        assert isinstance(other, SglExpr), f"{other}"
347	
348	        return self.concatenate_ir(other, self)
349	
350	    def concatenate_ir(self, a, b):
351	        if isinstance(a, SglExprList):
352	            if isinstance(b, SglExprList):
353	                return SglExprList(a.expr_list + b.expr_list)
354	            else:
355	                return SglExprList(a.expr_list + [b])
356	        elif isinstance(b, SglExprList):
357	            return SglExprList([a] + b.expr_list)
358	
359	        return SglExprList([a, b])
360	
361	    def print_graph_dfs(self):
362	        ret = [""]
363	        visited = set()
364	
365	        def dfs_print(x):
366	            if x is None or x in visited:
367	                return
368	            visited.add(x)
369	
370	            # Print dependency
371	            if x.prev_node is not None:
372	                dfs_print(x.prev_node)
373	
374	            if isinstance(x, SglExprList):
375	                for y in x.expr_list:
376	                    dfs_print(y)
377	            # elif isinstance(x, SglRole):
378	            #    dfs_print(x.expr)
379	            elif isinstance(x, SglVariable):
380	                dfs_print(x.source)
381	
382	            # Print the node itself
383	            if isinstance(x, (SglFork, SglGetForkItem)):
384	                ret[0] += f"%{x.node_id} = {x}\n"
385	            else:
386	                if x.prev_node is not None:
387	                    ret[0] += (
388	                        f"%{x.node_id} = %{x.prev_node.node_id} + " + str(x) + "\n"
389	                    )
390	                else:
391	                    ret[0] += f"%{x.node_id} = " + str(x) + "\n"
392	
393	        dfs_print(self)
394	        return ret[0]
395

466	        presence_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
467	        ignore_eos: Optional[bool] = None,
468	        return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
469	        logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
470	        top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
471	        return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
472	        dtype: Optional[type] = None,
473	        regex: Optional[str] = None,
474	        json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
475	    ):
476	        """Call the model to generate. See the meaning of the arguments in docs/backend/sampling_params.md"""
477	        super().__init__()
478	        self.name = name
479	        self.sampling_params = SglSamplingParams(
480	            max_new_tokens=max_new_tokens,
481	            min_new_tokens=min_new_tokens,
482	            n=n,
483	            stop=stop,
484	            stop_regex=stop_regex,
485	            stop_token_ids=stop_token_ids,
486	            temperature=temperature,
487	            top_p=top_p,
488	            top_k=top_k,
489	            min_p=min_p,
490	            frequency_penalty=frequency_penalty,
491	            presence_penalty=presence_penalty,
492	            ignore_eos=ignore_eos,
493	            return_logprob=return_logprob,
494	            logprob_start_len=logprob_start_len,
495	            top_logprobs_num=top_logprobs_num,
496	            return_text_in_logprobs=return_text_in_logprobs,
497	            dtype=dtype,
498	            regex=regex,
499	            json_schema=json_schema,
500	        )
501	
502	    def __repr__(self):
503	        return f"Gen('{self.name}')"
504	
505	
506	class SglConstantText(SglExpr):
507	    def __init__(self, value: str):
508	        super().__init__()
509	        self.value = value
510	
511	    def __repr__(self):
512	        return f"Constant({repr(self.value)})"

619	class SglSeparateReasoning(SglExpr):

621	        super().__init__()

623	
624	        self.expr = expr
625	        self.name = None
626	        self._process_expr(expr)
627	
628	    def process_name_for_reasoning(self, name):
629	        if not name:
630	            raise ValueError("name must be provided")
631	        return f"{name}_reasoning_content"
632	
633	    def _process_expr(self, expr):
634	        if isinstance(expr, SglGen):
635	            self.name = self.process_name_for_reasoning(expr.name)
636	        elif isinstance(expr, SglSelect):
637	            self.name = self.process_name_for_reasoning(expr.name)
638	        elif isinstance(expr, SglExprList):
639	            for x in expr.expr_list:
640	                self._process_expr(x)
641	
642	    def __repr__(self):
643	        return f"SeparateReasoning(model_type={self.model_type}, name={self.name})"
644
